A single well recorded airplane impact provides an empirical estimate for the energy coupling of surface ocean impacts to the SOFAR channel of 10$^{-4}$ for high velocity surface impacts.

title: Cross-Attentive Multiview Fusion of Vision-Language Embeddings
authors: Tomas Berriel Martins, Martin R. Oswald, Javier Civera
publish: arXiv
abstract: Vision-language models have been key to the development of open-vocabulary 2D semantic segmentation. Lifting these models from 2D images to 3D scenes, however, remains a challenging problem. Existing approaches typically back-project and average 2D descriptors across views, or heuristically select a single representative one, often resulting in suboptimal 3D representations. In this work, we introduce a novel multiview transformer architecture that cross-attends across vision-language descriptors from multiple viewpoints and fuses them into a unified per-3D-instance embedding. As a second contribution, we leverage multiview consistency as a self-supervision signal for this fusion, which significantly improves performance when added to a standard supervised target-class loss. Our Cross-Attentive Multiview Fusion, which we denote with its acronym CAMFusion, not only consistently outperforms naive averaging or single-view descriptor selection, but also achieves state-of-the-art results on 3D semantic and instance classification benchmarks, including zero-shot evaluations on out-of-domain datasets.

title: SEATrack: Simple, Efficient, and Adaptive Multimodal Tracker
authors: Junbin Su, Ziteng Xue, Shihui Zhang, Kun Chen, Weiming Hu, Zhipeng Zhang
publish: arXiv
abstract: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) in multimodal tracking reveals a concerning trend where recent performance gains are often achieved at the cost of inflated parameter budgets, which fundamentally erodes PEFT's efficiency promise. In this work, we introduce SEATrack, a Simple, Efficient, and Adaptive two-stream multimodal tracker that tackles this performance-efficiency dilemma from two complementary perspectives. We first prioritize cross-modal alignment of matching responses, an underexplored yet pivotal factor that we argue is essential for breaking the trade-off. Specifically, we observe that modality-specific biases in existing two-stream methods generate conflicting matching attention maps, thereby hindering effective joint representation learning. To mitigate this, we propose AMG-LoRA, which seamlessly integrates Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) for domain adaptation with Adaptive Mutual Guidance (AMG) to dynamically refine and align attention maps across modalities. We then depart from conventional local fusion approaches by introducing a Hierarchical Mixture of Experts (HMoE) that enables efficient global relation modeling, effectively balancing expressiveness and computational efficiency in cross-modal fusion. Equipped with these innovations, SEATrack advances notable progress over state-of-the-art methods in balancing performance with efficiency across RGB-T, RGB-D, and RGB-E tracking tasks. \href{https://github.com/AutoLab-SAI-SJTU/SEATrack}{\textcolor{cyan}{Code is available}}.

title: JWST observations of photodissociation regions. IV. Carbonaceous emission band sub-components in NGC 7023 have distinct spatial distributions
authors: D. Van De Putte, K. D. Gordon, K. Misselt, A. N. Witt, A. Abergel, A. Noriega-Crespo, P. Guillard, M. Zannese, M. Elyajouri, B. Trahin, P. Dell'ova, M. Baes, P. Klaassen
publish: arXiv
abstract: We analyze JWST spectroscopy of the northwest filament of NGC7023, where the relatively soft radiation field results in a photodissociation region with an extended atomic hydrogen region, and strongly pronounced variations of the carbonaceous emission band profiles. We focus on the 16.4 and 17.4 um bands and their relation to the main bands at 3.3, 3.4, 5.2, 5.7, 6.2, 7.7, 8.6, 11.3, and 12.7 um, and aim to identify which bands and sub-features originate from co-spatial emission carriers. We apply a PAHFIT spectral decomposition to measure the emission bands and their individual sub-components, and produce maps that spatially resolve the main dissociation front (DF1). Nearly all emission maps peak at DF1, while the relative intensity in the atomic hydrogen region (ATM) varies strongly. We classify the features into spatial distribution types based on the intensity ratio in ATM relative to DF1. Most bands are of type I (low ATM/DF1; 3.3, 3.4, 5.2, 5.7, 11.3 um) or II (medium ATM/DF1; 16.2, 7.7, 8.6, 12.7, 16.4 um), while only few are of type III (high ATM/DF1; 11.0, 17.4 um). A breakdown of the 5.7, 7.7, 11.3 and 12.7 um bands into blue and red sub-components reveals that contributions on the blue side are of type III, while those on the red side are of type I or II. These strongly differing spatial distributions reveal that at least two different populations contribute to the 16-18 um range, and that these populations are also connected to the profiles of the 5.7, 7.7, 11.3, and 12.7 um bands. The maps further indicate a continued evolution of these profiles toward the central cavity of NGC7023, where fullerene emission (C60) was previously detected. We speculate that the population of emission carriers could be in an intermediate photochemical evolution stage that precedes fullerene formation.

title: The parity operator for parafermions and parabosons
authors: N. I. Stoilova, J. Van der Jeugt
publish: arXiv
abstract: In this paper we reexamine the definition of parafermions and parabosons by means of Green's triple relations, and extend these relations by including a parity operator $P$ which is also determined by means of triple relations. As a consequence, we are dealing with new algebraic structures. It is shown that the algebra underlying a set of $n$ parafermions together with $P$ is the orthogonal Lie algebra $so(2n+2)$. The Fock spaces correspond to particular irreducible representations of $so(2n+2)$, and the action of $P$ in these spaces leads to interesting observations. Next, we show that the algebra underlying a set of $n$ parabosons together with $P$ is the orthosymplectic Lie superalgebra $osp(2|2n)$. In this case, the Fock spaces correspond to certain irreducible infinite-dimensional representations of $osp(2|2n)$. Both for parafermions and parabosons the spectrum of $P$ is closely related to the so-called order of statistics $p$, introduced by Green.

title: Stability and Geometry of Attractors in Neural Cellular Automata
authors: Mia-Katrin Kvalsund, James Stovold
publish: arXiv
abstract: Throughout the literature on Neural Cellular Automata (NCAs), it is often taken for granted that the systems learn attractors. This is shown through evolving the system for many timesteps and noting visual similarity to the goal state. There remain many questions after such an analysis. Namely, what kind of attractors do we have? Is their behavior ordered or chaotic? Can we estimate stability over very long time horizons? What really happens in the attractor when perturbations are applied? In this paper, we present a case study to help answer these questions, with methods drawn from the literature on dynamical systems theory. We use the growing gecko NCA of Mordvintsev et al. (2020) with deterministic cell updates as a case study. To the best of the authors' knowledge, we present the first visualizations of NCA attractor dynamics. We also analyze them using the Lyapunov and Fourier spectra, to reveal that the NCA displays oscillatory, periodic and quasi-periodic behavior, and that these behaviors arise early during training. This challenges the belief that NCAs learn fixed point attractors. Finally, we show that large perturbations to the attractor states can throw the NCAs into a secondary mode separate from the original attractor. We hope that this initial foray into NCA attractor dynamics expands the toolkit for NCA researchers to analyze the robustness and stability of their systems.

title: M1-92: AGB interruption and isotopic ratio paradox. Chemistry and morpho-kinematics from improved shapemol modelling
authors: E. Masa, J. Alcolea, M. Santander-García, V. Bujarrabal, C. Sánchez Contreras, A. Castro-Carrizo, W. Steffen, N. Koning
publish: arXiv
abstract: The shaping of planetary nebulae on their evolution from asymptotic giant branch circumstellar envelopes to their final, most often axisymmetrical, form is still a process with many unknown details. The key to understanding the whole shaping process is the study of the transition objects called pre-planetary nebulae (pPNe). In this context, modelling tools must be kept to the standard of radio telescope capabilities, so we can make the most of the data they collect. In this work we first present the newest update of the SHAPE and SHAPEMOL modelling tools, adding ten new molecular species to be reproduced together with other general improvements. Later, we put this new update into practice to study M1-92, a pPN with a rich chemistry that can provide valuable information on its origin and shaping. We created a 3D morpho-kinematical model of the nebula in SHAPE that is able to reproduce 23 line profiles from the IRAM 30m telescope and HIFI/HSO and five maps from IRAM NOEMA. The observational dataset is reproduced simultaneously under the same physical conditions, adjusting only the relative abundance of the different species. We obtained a full description of the nebula's physical and chemical properties, and we provide the total estimates for mass (0.79 $M_\odot$), linear momentum (4.10$\times10^{39}$ g cm s$^{-1}$), and kinetic energy (6.48$\times10^{45}$ erg) as well as their detailed distribution across the nebula. We also analysed the isotopic ratios, finding robust discrepancies (values of 10 versus 30) in the $^{12}$C/$^{13}$C ratio across structures depending on their age.

title: Scalable Qumode-Qubit State Transfer and Fast-forward Quantum Fourier Transform using Oscillators
authors: Joel Bierman, Shubdeep Mohapatra, Huiyang Zhou, Yuan Liu
publish: arXiv
abstract: Transferring the information stored in the expansion coefficients of a multi-qubit state to the coefficients of a continuous-variable state is an important protocol for communicating quantum information. It was shown in previous work how to transfer an $n$-qubit state to a single qumode in $\mathcal{O}(2^n)$ time. We show that by transferring this state to $m$ qumodes, the runtime can be improved to $\mathcal{O}(2^{n/m})$. Furthermore, we demonstrate how multi-qumode state transfer can be used as a subroutine for approximately realizing the $n$-qubits quantum Fourier transform on $m$-qumode with runtime scaling $\mathcal{O}(m2^{n/m}/ε+m^2)$, accelerating qubit quantum Fourier transform using qumodes. This work presents a scalable approach to convert discrete and continuous quantum information between an arbitrary number of qubits and qumodes. It represents a crucial step forward in mixed analog-digital quantum signal processing for computing, sensing, and communication.

title: Noncommutative differential geometry of ambiskew polynomial rings
authors: Andrés Rubiano, Armando Reyes
publish: arXiv
abstract: We determine sufficient criteria for the differential smoothness of ambiskew polynomial rings defined and studied by D. A. Jordan in several papers \cite{FishJordan2019, Jordan1993b, Jordan2000, JordanWells2013}.

title: Primordial Black Holes Formation Beyond the Standard Cosmic QCD Transition
authors: Maël Gonin, Oleksii Ivanytskyi, David Blaschke, Günther Hasinger
publish: arXiv
abstract: We review the role of primordial black holes (PBHs) for illuminating the dark ages of the cosmological evolution and as dark matter (DM) candidates. We elucidate the role of phase transitions for primordial black hole formation in the early Universe and focus our attention on the cosmological QCD phase transition within a recent microscopical model. We explore the impact of physics beyond the Standard Model (SM) on the cosmic equation of state and the probability distribution for the formation of PBHs which serve as candidates for DM and contribute to present-day binary black-hole merger events.

title: Neutrinoless double-beta decay of the $Δ^-$ resonance
authors: Li-Ping He, Feng-Kun Guo, Ulf-G. Meißner, De-Liang Yao, Xiao-Yu Zhang, Zhen-Hua Zhang
publish: arXiv
abstract: The subprocess $nn\to ppe^-e^-$ is a key ingredient in the interpretation of nuclear neutrinoless double-beta decay. Intermediate $Δ$ resonances may provide additional enhancements to this transition. We take a first step toward a $Δ$-full description of $nn\to ppe^-e^-$ by investigating the neutrinoless double-beta decay $Δ^- \to p e^-e^-$ in the framework of chiral effective field theory. We systematically derive the long-range contribution from light-Majorana-neutrino exchange through loop diagrams and incorporate the short-range part through counterterms required by renormalization. We predict the pion-mass dependence of the decay amplitude in the kinematic configuration with collinear electrons. Furthermore, to facilitate lattice-QCD matching, we calculate the decay amplitude in the degenerate $Δ$-nucleon mass limit and provide the corresponding long-range prediction.

title: Forecast-Driven Gait Cycle Analysis and Performance Monitoring Using a CNN-Based Intelligent Framework
authors: Kusumba Jyoshna Devi, Rajchandar. K
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Gait cycle analysis is essential to detect movement disorders, evaluate human performance, and provide a platform for early intervention in both clinical and non-clinical environments. In this paper, we propose a novel application of CNN using predictions for fine-grain gait cycle analysis and characterizing the performance in various populations. It integrates real-time sensor feedback with video-based motion capture to recognize important gait parameters like joint angles, step length, cadence, and phase transitions. These parameters are converted into Trimmed Gait Energy Images (TGEIs) and used as input to a special deep Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for classifying spatial-temporal patterns. Using a temporal prediction module, the model detects both existing and future variations of gait behaviors and can subsequently enable early monitoring and recognize potential intervention opportunities. Experiments As part of this research, we conducted a series of large-scale experiments with population-level data from different demographic groups (athletes, the elderly, and people with neurological or orthopaedic conditions). The results demonstrate that the system achieves more than 95% accuracy in fault diagnosis and offers superior performance prediction to classical biomechanical models and other simple machine learning algorithms.

title: High-Resolution Spectral Construction and Separation from Low-Cost, Low-Resolution Sensor Data
authors: Zhiming Gou, Hao Sha, Xinwei Gao, Wouter R. Ryckaert, Frédéric B. Leloup
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Accurate monitoring of personal light exposure is crucial for investigating non-visual effects of light. However, low-cost wearable light loggers often lack essential features, such as a sufficiently high spectral resolution or precise tracking capabilities. In this study, a Transformer framework for spectral reconstruction and separation from multichannel sensor data is introduced. A dual-stream Transformer model is proposed, which reconstructs and decomposes high-resolution, all-day mixed-light spectral distributions. The model uses self-attention to decouple the mixed temporal dynamics of daylight and artificial light, guided by a composite loss function for physical consistency. Results show excellent numerical and colorimetric fidelity and accurately reconstruct the spectral power distribution to track key metrics. As a result, this work establishes an initial framework for personal spectral dosimetry, enabling low-cost data collection to advance temporal studies on non-visual effects of light.

title: Smart Energy Monitoring: A Case Study on PLC Based Data Acquisition and Python Analytics
authors: S.N.V.S.K. Chaitanya, Pusalapati Yuva Chakri, Lanka Shanmukha Sai, Koppula Praveen Kumar, Penumaka Santhosh, P.S.D.S. Badarinath
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Environmentally safe operation and energy efficiency are ever more important in educational institutions. This platform, which relies on PLCs and Python-based analysis tools, monitors energy usage in real-time in most departments, which uncover opportunities for future consumption and peak usage intervals. The objective is to provide a scalable platform that will be capable of recognizing trends and deviations in use of power Electrical measurement is performed by a data collection system from various campus locations and stored in a single, centralized database to be analyzed. This pre-emptive move can lead to greater operational efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and to guarantee sustainability. In our project, we compared the power utilization of the Civil and the EEE blocks. The power consumption was lower in the Civil block with traditional spaces classrooms but within the EEE block with technology-enriched classrooms. By seeing to it that some optimization methods, these results encourage sustainability initiatives and serve as a model to other academic institutions.

title: Tracking the Dieback of Palm and Olive Groves in The Skoura Oase (Morocco): A Spatio-Temporal Approach Using Remote Sensing and Machine Learning
authors: Youssef Lassiane, Farid El Wahidi, Hoda Benazun, Hassan Ait Naceur
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: Desertification remains one of the most pressing environmental challenges in arid and semi-arid regions, undermining ecosystem resilience and agricultural productivity. In southern Morocco, oasis landscapes constitute highly fragile socio-ecological systems where vegetation dieback has intensified under the combined influence of climatic stress and human pressure. This study explores the spatio-temporal dynamics of palm and olive tree dieback in the Skoura Oasis (Morocco) from 2015 to 2025, using multi-temporal Sentinel-2 imagery processed on the Google Earth Engine (GEE) platform. A Random Forest algorithm was applied to tasseled cap transformation (TCT) components-Brightness, Greenness, and Wetness-to extract ecological indicators related to vegetation vigor and surface moisture. The model achieved an overall accuracy exceeding 89%, confirming its robustness in discriminating vegetation health conditions. Results indicate a marked acceleration in palm and olive dieback during the last decade, with the southern sections of the palmeraie exhibiting the most severe losses. In the central oasis, a persistent and continuous dieback pattern was also detected. These findings reveal the alarming pace of ecosystem deterioration across the Draa oases, reflecting the second agro-ecological crisis driven by progressive aridification and groundwater depletion. This research demonstrates the high potential of integrating Sentinel-based remote sensing with machine learning for long-term ecological monitoring, providing critical insights for sustainable oasis management and the preservation of traditional agroforestry systems in dryland environments.

title: Swarm Robotics for Precision Agriculture: Overcoming Coordination Scalability Limits
authors: Loso Judijanto, Sri Nurhayati
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: The rapid evolution of precision agriculture demands innovative approaches to enhance productivity, efficiency, and environmental sustainability. Among these, swarm robotics emerges as a promising paradigm, offering decentralized coordination, fault tolerance, and adaptability for large-scale farming operations. However, significant coordination and scalability limitations continue to hinder its real-world deployment, particularly in heterogeneous and dynamic agricultural environments. This study aims to systematically explore existing strategies to overcome coordination and scalability challenges in swarm robotics for precision agriculture. A Systematic Literature Review (SLR) was conducted using the PRISMA protocol, focusing on peerreviewed articles published between 2021 and 2025. The research employed a qualitative meta-synthesis approach to extract themes, models, and performance outcomes across multiple studies. Data were sourced exclusively from the ScienceDirect database, covering peer-reviewed articles published between 2021 and 2025. The search process involved keyword combinations such as “swarm robotics,” “coordination,” “scalability,” and “precision agriculture.” After screening and eligibility checks, 36 articles were selected based on open-access availability and methodological clarity. Thematic analysis was applied to extract coordination strategies, simulation validations, energy management approaches, and integration patterns. Findings indicate that hierarchical coordination models, biologically inspired algorithms (e.g., ACO, PSO), and hybrid communication protocols are the most effective in addressing scalability.

title: Dynamic Calibration Method of Torque Sensor Based on Parameter Identification Technology
authors: Xu Zhou, Qiliang Dai, Fangjian Yuan, Hang Xiu, Lei Wang, Yu Li, YuanPeng Lv
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This study presents an advanced vertical torsion pendulum system for dynamic torque sensor calibration, featuring an optimized gravity-free design that ensures exceptional measurement stability. The system employs a sophisticated four-degree-of-freedom dynamic model to precisely characterize torsional behavior through stiffness, damping, and inertia matrices. Our innovative calibration methodology integrates experimental measurements with theoretical simulations using a dual-input excitation approach, enhanced by a differential evolution algorithm that systematically optimizes model parameters within physically constrained bounds. Experimental validation demonstrates outstanding performance, achieving merely 0.11% calibration error (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.1 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{N} \cdot \mathrm{m}$</tex>) at 3.2% parameter accuracy - a significant improvement over the 8.2% error observed at 16.4% accuracy. This physics-based, traceable calibration solution not only surpasses conventional static methods but also provides reliable in-situ performance evaluation, addressing critical industrial metrology requirements for modern measurement and control systems across diverse sensor configurations and operational conditions.

title: Detecting PMSM Current-Sensor Offset Faults from the Battery Current Signature
authors: Ciro Attaianese, Matilde D'Arpino, Mauro Di Monaco, Luigi Pio Di Noia
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Driving and Smart Transportation (IDST)
abstract: In the paper, a model for detection of current sensors offset fault in field-oriented controlled permanent magnet synchronous machine (PMSM) drives is proposed. The model allows for isolating and detecting the current sensor offset of PMSM drives using the signature of battery pack current. In previous papers, the authors proposed an analytical model capable of determining the d-q currents in a PMSM drive affected by offset faults using the phase-measured currents and the drive parameters. In this paper, the detection of offset fault is carried out using the current of the battery pack feeding the PMSM drives. The goodness of the proposed solution is verified through numerical simulations.

title: Dual Boost Modified DC-DC Converter for Fuel Cell Fed PMSM Application
authors: Rajesh Uppara, Phani Teja Bankupalli, G Y Rajaa Vikhram
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: A hydrogen fuel cell driving a Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSM) have been promising drive train for Electric vehicle (EV) systems. This paper emphases on the integration and validation of a modified dual boost DC/DC converter (DBMDC) for PMSM fed to Polymer Electrolyte Membrane Fuel Cell (PEMFC). The dual boost converter is developed by cascading conventional boost converter (CBC) to a modified boost converter, whose input is fed by low voltage PEMFC stack. To overcome the inherent limitation of sluggish PEMF response, an auxiliary source (battery) is connected to the DC bus through a bidirectional converter. On comparison to traditional boost converter operating at same duty cycle, the DBMDC provides a greater voltage gain using two stage operation, while reducing power loss during energy conversion.

title: Analysis of Solar Based Domestic Induction Heating Using Class D Converter
authors: Anand Kumar, Anik Goswami, Ajay Kumar Maurya
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Solar energy is increasingly utilized around the world for generating electricity, as well as for heating and desalinating water. This paper analyzes a solar-powered home induction heating (IH) system that employs a Class D converter. The objective is to develop an off-grid, sustainable heating system that minimizes reliance on traditional power sources, utilizing electromagnetic induction (EMI) for efficient heat generation. The primary power source within the system architecture is a photovoltaic (PV) solar panel, followed by a DC-DC boost converter that increases the voltage to the necessary level. A Class D converter then receives this boosted DC voltage and converts it into the high-frequency alternating current (HF-AC) required for induction heating. An RLC (Resistor-Inductor-Capacitor) load simulates the behavior of the heating coil and the heated material during the induction heating process. The main goals of this project were to ensure efficient power conversion, generate HF-AC output, and demonstrate the feasibility of using solar energy for induction heating. The analysis shows the potential of this technology as an environmentally friendly alternative for home heating applications and confirms that the specified goals have been successfully achieved through simulation.

title: Smart Illumination: Utilizing AI for Early Fault Detection in Lighting Systems
authors: Adam Bouras, Mohamed Wassim Fekih, Zouhour Araoud
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: In smart buildings, lighting systems are essential infrastructure; if they fail, the result is safety risks, wasted energy, and operational disruptions. Traditional and reactive maintenance-fixing equipment only after it breaks-often leads to expensive emergency repairs and significant downtime. This paper introduces an AI-based predictive maintenance framework for intelligent lighting that can forecast failures days or even weeks before they occur. By continuously tracking variables such as power usage, brightness, temperature, and flicker, our machine learning models identify subtle anomalies that signal a coming fault. Tested over eight months in a real-world building with 240 lights, our ensemble learning models (Random Forest and Gradient Boosting) achieved 91% detection accuracy with nearly a week (6.8 days) of advance warning. Implementation of this system: Cut unplanned downtime by 73%. Reduced maintenance expenses by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 2 \%}$</tex>. Boosted overall system reliability from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4. 2 \%}$</tex> to 98.7%. The framework integrates directly with DALI-2 digital lighting standards, providing clear, actionable data for precise maintenance scheduling.

title: A Novel Fastening Device with Precision Adjustment for Drainage Lines in Electric Power Systems: Precision-Adjustable Fastener for Power System Drainage Lines
authors: Mian Pei, Bo Mei, Zhiping Zhang, Jie Sheng, Yunfei Yang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper introduces an Actively Controlled Drainage Line Fastening Device (ADLFD) that dynamically modulates the electrical impedance of transmission line shield wire connections to enhance power system transient stability. The device transitions a traditionally passive mechanical component into an actively controlled element within the system's protection framework. When fault is detected, the ADLFD quickly reduces tower connection point contact resistance, significantly reducing drainage path impedance. This effectively abates recurrent secondary arcs-the predominant cause of auto-reclose failure. Stricter electromagnetic transient (EMT) and stability modeling confirms the ADLFD effectiveness, indicating a 90% reduction in impedance compared to aged traditional clamps. This performance equates to dramatic real-world improvement: autoreclose success rates shoot up from 8 % to 95 %, and the clearing time on critical events increases by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 2. 6 \%}$</tex>. These outcomes place the ADLFD as a revolutionary hardware solution that materially enhances grid resilience against transient instability.

title: Fault Diagnosis and Identification for Stator Winding Faults of Induction Motor using Wavelet-Alienation based Algorithm
authors: Ravi Soni, Nandkishor Gupta, Bhuvnesh Rathore
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: This work presents a robust protection scheme for diagnosis and type detection of faults in 3-phase squirrel cage induction motors (SCIM). In the suggested method, 3-phase signals are processed and analyzed using discrete wavelet transform (DWT) to extract the approximate coefficients, which are subsequently utilized to compute the alienation coefficients through correlation analysis. These alienation coefficients act as crucial indicators for stator fault diagnosis and classification; by comparing their magnitudes against a predefined threshold. The implemented algorithm exhibits high levels of accuracy, reliability and computational efficiency in diagnosing and assessing various stator fault conditions in three-phase SCIMs.

title: Fast and Memory-Efficient 4SQR-Code Decoding Using Histogram Analysis
authors: Md. Shadikur Shadikur Rahman Sheam, Md Roman Nihal, Shariful Islam Emon, Md. Romzan Alom, Kabiratun Ummi Oyshe, Muhammad Aminur Rahaman, A B M Shawkat Ali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: With the demand for high-capacity yet compact encoding systems in the Internet of Things, logistics, and digital documentation, there is a need for a new format. Hence, the Four State Quick Response (4SQR) Code format is a promising alternative to traditional binary QR codes. It doubles the storage capacity by representing data using four distinct geometric shapes. However, adding two new shapes introduced decoding challenges. This paper proposes a histogram-based decoding method for 4SQR-Code that reduces both decoding time and memory usage. The proposed methodology includes several preprocessing steps: grayscale conversion, cropping, and patch extraction, followed by histogram calculation with data normalization and flattening, and correlation analysis. It maps the shapes directly to binary values and then to Unicode Transformation Format, 8-bit (UTF8). Experimental results reveal significant improvements of the proposed method compared to the existing decoder. The average decoding time was reduced from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5,982.83$</tex> milliseconds to 79.97 milliseconds, while the average memory usage decreased from 15.6 megabytes to 1.73 megabytes for 3,000 test samples. These results prove the efficiency and practicality of the proposed method. With this decoding method, the applicability of the 4SQR-Code increased because low latency and constrained memory are critical in real-world scenarios.

title: Exploring Factors Affecting Entrepreneurs' Intention towards Adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI): Using an Extended TOE Framework
authors: Arif Hasan, Navita Nathani, Vikas Kumar Khare, Sandeep Raghuwanshi, Umesh Gautam
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: The present study explores the application of AI by Indian entrepreneurs as per Technology-Organisation-Environment (TOE) based framework. Being one of the approaches to combining qualitative and quantitative methods, it considers how AI is improving the business processes, decision-making and efficiency in the service, trade and manufacturing sectors. The empirical data were examined through Exploratory Factor Analysis (EFA), followed by Confirmatory Factor Analysis (CFA) and Structural Equation Modelling (SEM), employing SPSS 18 and AMOS 20 for statistical analysis. Previous results indicate, Artificial intelligence is enhancing the accuracy of the business and livelihood of the people as it can predict the information and the market. Policy recommendations and practical advice should be circulated to popularise AI among entrepreneurs. It suggests in drafting the importance of technological, organisational and environmental stress launching the AI as a Platform to draft the Programmes and policies can be made to support and facilitate economic growth.

title: Research on Deep Learning-Based Methods for Detecting Broken Strand Defects in Ground Wires
authors: Shiqiang Yang, Xiaolai Li, Xiangkui He, Xinwei Feng, Yuxin Wu, Xiaying Xiong
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the challenges of high dynamic range, complex backgrounds, and variable illumination in images, this paper first proposes an improved YOLO-based enhancement algorithm to improve robustness under these conditions. Next, an RDN-based super-resolution reconstruction module is introduced to enhance the detail features in low-resolution images. ly, experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can reliably detect transmission line conductors and their defects (strand looseness and breakage) in various environments, achieving an overall performance metric of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4. 8 \%}$</tex>.

title: FedFall: A Federated Transfer Learning Framework for Privacy-Aware Fall Detection in Elderly Care using IoT-Edge Devices
authors: Abdulrahman K. Alnaim, Khalied M. Albarrak, Abdullah A. Alnuaim
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Falls are a leading cause of injury, hospitalization, and mortality among elderly individuals, posing significant challenges to healthcare systems worldwide. Traditional fall detection systems often rely on centralized cloud processing, which introduces latency, privacy concerns, and limited adaptability to individual users. To address these limitations, we propose FedFall, a novel federated and transfer learning-based framework for real-time, privacy-pre-serving fall detection on IoT-edge devices. FedFall enables on-device inference and personalization by fine-tuning a pretrained base model using user-specific data while pre-serving raw data locality. The framework supports secure model aggregation through federated averaging and integrates optional differential privacy to enhance confidentiality. Designed for low-power wearable devices, FedFall achieves efficient deployment without compromising responsiveness or accuracy. We validate the framework using standard activity recognition datasets and simulated edge-client scenarios, demonstrating its feasibility in delivering accurate, adaptive, and privacy-conscious fall detection across heterogeneous edge environments.

title: Coordinated Interaction Strategy of Source-Grid-Load-Storage Based on Transformer Stability
authors: Junhong Chen, Qiran Zhang, Zhaobin Zhong, Wenzhen Zhang, Bochuan Gu, Weiting Li
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: In the process of constructing a new power system, the multi-subject and multi-dimensional interactive relationship between Source-Grid-Load-Storage has become increasingly complex. As the core hub equipment for power transmission and distribution, the operational stability of transformers is facing the superimposed impacts of sudden power changes caused by large-scale integration of new energy, instantaneous current surges during energy storage charging and discharging, and random fluctuations in user-side loads. Traditional Source-Grid-Load-Storage coordination strategies mostly focus on optimizing overall system benefits, making it difficult to achieve efficient allocation of system resources while ensuring the safe and stable operation of transformers. To address this issue, This paper proposes a set of collaborative interaction strategies: first, by constructing a high-precision Source-Grid-Load-Storage resource characteristic model, the operating laws of various elements under transformer stability constraints are accurately depicted; second, with the safe and stable operation of transformers as a hard constraint, the operating costs and benefit demands of power source, grid, load, and energy storage subjects are comprehensively balanced, and an effective incentive mechanism is established to mobilize the enthusiasm of all subjects for collaborative participation; meanwhile, a distributed coordinated control platform is developed to realize the observability, measurability, controllability, and optimal dispatching of Source-Grid-Load-Storage resources.

title: Integrating Deep Reinforcement Learning with AI Algorithms for Optimal Path Planning of Indoor Handling Robots
authors: Jinran Song
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Indoor handling robots play a critical role in automated warehousing, manufacturing workshop material transfer, and other industrial scenarios, where path planning directly affects operational efficiency, safety, and energy consumption. Traditional path planning algorithms face inherent limitations: global algorithms (e.g., <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithm) excel in finding shortest paths based on static maps but lack adaptability to dynamic obstacles (e.g., temporary goods, pedestrians); local algorithms (e.g., fuzzy logic) enable real-time adjustments but tend to fall into local optima; and single deep reinforcement learning (DRL) methods, while adaptive to dynamic environments, struggle with global path optimality and require extensive training. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a hybrid path planning method integrating the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithm (as the AI-based global planner) and DRL (as the local dynamic adjuster). For global path planning, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithm uses a Manhattan-distance heuristic function to generate the shortest path based on preknown indoor maps (shelf layouts, passage distributions), providing a foundational navigation framework. For local dynamic adaptation, a DRL module (based on Deep Q-Network, DQN) is designed: the robot acts as an agent, with state inputs including its position, heading angle, and dynamic obstacle information; the reward function balances obstacle avoidance, path tracking, and operational efficiency; and the agent learns real-time obstacle avoidance strategies through training. Experiments were conducted in both simulated (ROS/Gazebo) and real-world warehouse environments, comparing the proposed method with single <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A^{*}$</tex>, single DRL, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A^{*}+$</tex> fuzzy logic algorithms. Results show that the proposed method increases dynamic obstacle avoidance success rate by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 \% - 2 0 \%}$</tex>, shortens path execution time by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 \%}-\mathbf{1 2 \%}$</tex>, and controls path length deviation within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 \%}$</tex> compared to baseline methods. This work effectively balances global optimality and dynamic adaptability, meeting the requirements of automated warehousing and workshop material transfer scenarios.

title: An Explainable Machine Learning Approach for Agricultural Crop Recommendation
authors: Ankit Jaiswal, Pushpendra Kumar, Aryan Patel, Gaurav Singh, Anil Kumar Yadav
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Multimedia, Signal Processing and Communication Technologies (IMPACT)
abstract: Crop selection is a pivotal decision-making process for farmers, impacting cultivation outcomes and the efficient allocation of resources. Traditionally, crop selection has been based on farmers' experience and local knowledge. However, under dynamic environmental conditions such as soil quality, climate change, and weather variability, this traditional approach makes it difficult to make well-informed decisions. This paper introduces “Harvestify,” a crop recommendation system that combines predictive accuracy and interpretability. Harvestify in-corporates various machine learning (ML) techniques, including Random Forest, Support Vector Machines, K-Nearest Neighbors, LightGBM, and XGBoost, and rigorously evaluates their crop prediction capabilities. Comparative analysis revealed that the XGBoost classifier demonstrated superior performance, leading to its adoption as the implemented model. To ensure transparency, the SHAP (Shapley Additive explanations) approach is introduced to generate interpretable explanations for crop recommendations based on various soil and climate parameters. The SHAP approach is an interpretability framework based on game theory, assigning a unique value to each feature in the dataset, indicating the contribution of each feature to the model's prediction. This research demonstrates the synergistic integration of machine learning techniques and explainable artificial intelligence (AI) for precision agriculture, providing data-driven insights to farmers while maintaining transparency in decision-making.

title: Design and Implementation of a Line Array CMOS High-Speed Acquisition System Using FPGA
authors: Bing Li, Linxian Liu, Jia Gao, Yang Xu, Xuehai Qiao, Wenjie Liang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a high-speed acquisition system for linear CMOS sensors based on an FPGA. The system utilizes the Hamamatsu S11108 linear CMOS as the image sensor. An FPGA chip and Verilog HDL are employed to implement overall system control. The output signal from the CMOS is sampled and converted by an AD9826 A/D converter, with the resultant data transmitted at high speed via a USB 3.0 interface. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves high-speed acquisition for 2048 pixels at a frame rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4,670 \text{fps}$</tex>. The system exhibits high frame rates, high sensitivity, stable performance, and portability, and portability. It also offers a degree of versatility, providing reliable low-level support for applications such as spectral analysis, industrial inspection, and precision measurement.

title: Early Detection of Varicose Veins Using Vision Transformers in Deep Learning
authors: J. M. Jenilyn, V.Mulli Mary, M. Jasmine Mary, Y. Mary Jansi Rani, C. Jasphin
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Varicose veins, a prevalent vascular disorder, are commonly observed among individuals engaged in prolonged standing occupations such as teachers, conductors, and retail workers. Early detection is crucial to prevent severe complications like chronic pain and venous ulcers. However, conventional diagnostic methods are manual and time- consuming. This paper proposes an automated approach for early varicose vein detection using Vision Transformers. Our method leverages self-attention mechanisms to efficiently classify medical images of lower limbs. The experimental results show that the ViT model significantly outperforms traditional classifiers like Support Vector Machines and Convolutional Neural Networks in terms of accuracy and scalability, achieving 95.8% accuracy. The model was deployed in a web-based application, making early screening accessible to a broader population.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Multi-Dimensional Trust Evolution and Adversarial Resilience Mechanisms in Flying Ad Hoc Networks
authors: Sumit Kumar, Jay Prakash
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Flying Ad Hoc Networks (FANETs) are increasingly used for critical missions that need secure and reliable multi-UAV collaboration. However, their open wireless links and changing topologies make them vulnerable to various insider and external attacks. Traditional cryptographic methods alone are not enough to identify compromised or misbehaving UAVs. In this work, we introduce a new blockchain-based, multi-dimensional trust evolution framework to improve security and resilience in FANETs. Each UAV maintains a trust vector consisting of five behavioral aspects: Packet Delivery Ratio, Link Stability, Cryptographic Compliance, Mobility Consistency, and Energy Behavior. Trust dynamics are modeled with Kalman filter smoothing and Bayesian inference for detecting sophisticated adversaries like on-off, collusion, and Sybil attackers. Trust updates are stored on a lightweight blockchain for tamper-proof, auditable trust dissemination. The framework is implemented using NS-3 for UAV network simulation and Python for trust evaluation and blockchain integration. Results show that our approach achieves high attack detection rates (over 95%), quick trust convergence, and low overhead, while greatly enhancing network reliability during attacks. The proposed framework offers an effective, scalable, and auditable trust management solution for nextgeneration secure FANET deployments. This study supports SDG 9, SDG 11 of the UN Sustainable Development Goals.

title: Adaptive Window-Length Adjustment and Feature Fusion for Enhanced CFAR Detection
authors: Zhongli Zhang, Xuezhen Cheng
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper proposes an APSO-CFAR algorithm incorporating multi-feature fusion discrimination and adaptive window length adjustment. The traditional Cell Averaging CFAR (CA-CFAR) tends to have an increased detection threshold due to interfering targets in multi-target environments, while also suffering from a sharp increase in false alarms at clutter edges. Although the Smallest Of CFAR (SO-CFAR) alleviates multi-target interference to a certain extent, it still has the issue of relatively high false alarms at clutter edges. The proposed method in this paper constructs a more discriminative fused detection metric by integrating multi-dimensional features such as clutter power, target distance, and velocity, thereby enhancing the robustness of target representation. Meanwhile, it dynamically optimizes the reference window length based on local variance: a larger window length is adopted in homogeneous backgrounds to improve estimation accuracy, while the window length is shrunk at interference or clutter edges to suppress interference from non-homogeneous samples. Experimental results show that the proposed algorithm significantly improves multi-target detection capability and adaptability to clutter edges while maintaining a stable false alarm rate.

title: AI Regulation in the U.S.: Lessons from Commodity Futures Legislative Journey and a Perspective CFTC-NFA Model for AI
authors: Di Kevin Gao, Jingdao Chen, Shahram Rahimi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: The emergence of ChatGPT in November 2022 and the subsequent rush to “AI-ize” everything from scheduling to speech synthesis to surgery have decisively shifted the focus of AI regulation from “if” to “what” and “how”. While other major economies, especially the European Union, have taken the lead in AI regulation, substantial disagreements on what prospective AI regulation should look like have inhibited necessary progress in the United States. It is critically important for the AI community to coalesce around a shared vision to advance AI regulation effectively. To that end, the authors examined the US legislative history for insights. After extensive research, we found an equally disruptive and controversial parallel: commodity futures. Futures contract, a 19th-century invention, was initially hailed by growers, buyers, and bankers as a breakthrough. Its legislative journey, however, was tumultuous. It was almost banned nationwide, as anti-option laws passed both chambers of Congress, but was ultimately spared when Congress failed to reconcile the House and Senate bills. In the next 100 years, Congress experimented and patch-worked various versions of futures laws and finally settled on a principles-based, central-agency-managed, and self-regulatory organization (SRO)-supported regulation, facilitating its exponential growth. Demonstrating their adaptability, commodity futures laws have become a primary vehicle for regulating emerging digital assets such as cryptocurrencies. In this article, the authors analyze the similarities between the legislative experience of AI and futures: both face an emotional and opinionated general public, a slow-moving Congress, and active state legislatures. Futures' treacherous legislative journey, near-death experiences, and subsequent astronomical growth provide AI with a cautious yet promising roadmap. At the conclusion of this article, the authors recommend a principles-based law, central-agency-led architect, and SRO-supported model to regulate AI, supported by AI regulatory enablers including AI registration and disclosure, AI monitoring, and AI enforcement.

title: Radiative Cooling for Enhanced Efficiency in Outdoor LED Lighting Systems
authors: Guillermo del Campo, Igor Gomez, Edgar Saavedra, Asuncion Santamaria
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Radiative cooling (RC) materials are emerging as a promising passive strategy to lower the operating temperature by combining high solar reflectance with strong mid–infrared emissivity. This paper presents an experimental assessment of a commercial RC film, with and without compound parabolic concentrator (CPC) optics, applied to a commercial LED street luminaire under realistic outdoor conditions. Different configurations were evaluated: full <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R C}$</tex> coverage of the luminaire head, partial RC strips over critical zones (LED and driver), and RC combined with 2D CPC elements mounted above <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R C}$</tex>–coated regions. Surface temperatures were measured at multiple points on the luminaire, together with ambient temperature and solar irradiance, during both daytime and nighttime operation. RC film consistently reduced luminaire surface temperatures during daytime compared with the reference configuration, with typical reductions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7-8^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> and peak differences of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$13{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> approaching under strong irradiance. Partial RC coverage still yielded local cooling of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2-3^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> degrees in critical regions, while requiring less material and having lower impact on the product form factor. CPCs preserved or slightly enhanced the RC effect, acting as incremental modifiers rather than primary drivers. The study also discusses material characteristics (durability, mechanical properties), environmental robustness of CPC geometries, and preliminary cost–benefit considerations. The results are intentionally exploratory, but they provide system–level evidence that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R C}$</tex> and RC+CPC integration is feasible and beneficial for LED street luminaires, and they establish a quantitative baseline for future optimization and long–term studies in smart–city lighting applications.

title: Global Path Planning for Unmanned Surface Vehicles Based on Line-of-Sight Guidance
authors: Xing Liu, Jianyi Li, Qinghua Huo, Bing Yang, Kali Feng
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: With the development of marine resources and the increasing demand for maritime missions, as an important marine operation tool, the global path planning technology of unmanned surface vehicles has become a research hotspot. This study aims to design an efficient and safe global path planning method for unmanned surface vehicles to improve their mission execution capabilities in complex marine environments. Based on the line-of-sight guidance planning method, by simulating the line-of-sight guidance method of human drivers, an optimal path from the starting point to the end point is planned for the unmanned surface vehicle. This method combines the line segment and arc segment guidance methods, which is not only simple in calculation and strong in real-time performance, but also can effectively cope with the path planning needs in different marine environments. The research finds that the global path planning method of unmanned surface vehicles based on line-of-sight guidance has significantly improved the efficiency and safety of unmanned surface vehicle mission execution, and provided strong technical support for the development of unmanned surface vehicle technology.

title: BhashaHMPV: Multilingual HMPV News Articles Dataset for Indian Regional Languages
authors: Jigyasa Verma, Shakshi Sharma, Rahul Goel
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: In this work, we present a multi-lingual dataset of news stories that were gathered by online automation from Google News. The majority of infodemic analysis research focuses on datasets in the English language, however, our study bridges a significant gap by taking into account a variety of linguistic situations. Several Indian languages, including Hindi, Punjabi, Gujarati, Tamil, Telugu, Bengali, Marathi, Malayalam, and Urdu, are represented in the dataset's news items. Understanding how false information and stories about the Human Metapneumovirus (HMPV) disseminate across languages is crucial given India's rich linguistic variety. Language identifiers, full-length content, article sources, and links to original news stories are all included in the dataset's comprehensive metadata. In order to gain a better understanding of how HMPV is addressed in various geographical contexts, we employ topic modeling tools to extract prevalent themes across languages. Additionally, we perform descriptive analysis by looking at commonly used phrases, social media references, and myths related to HMPV. This dataset is a useful tool for multi-lingual NLP applications, public health communication research, and misinformation detection. The dataset has been released for public use<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>1https://zenodo.org/records/15194116.

title: Analyzing Bitcoin's Intraday Variance Using LSTM, GARCH and Hybrid Model
authors: K. Vanitha, Adarsh S, Ayyanar M, Abinash B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Bitcoin and cryptocurrencies have become a hip financial instrument that is extremely volatile with disconnected trading. The current paper investigates the intraday volatility of Bitcoin based on econometric and datadriven models to capture short-term changes in volatility. The paper introduces a graphical method of data analytics in the form of the heatmap that is used to identify clusters of volatility and time dynamic in the trading day. Using GARCHtype volatility models and advanced machine learning techniques with LSTM-GARCH hybrids, the system estimates and display changing market behavior based on 1-minute and 5-minute tick data from Digital Currencies 2024. The results confirm the existence of a great deal of intraday heterogeneity, which is provided by the convergence of the global market and the investing spirit, and which provides guidance to traders, analysts, and policymakers in forecasting risks and microstructure market behavior. Performance measures found that the hybrid framework had the lowest MAE (0.025), RMSE (0.053), and MSE (0.002809), and the highest R2 of 0.91.

title: Optimized Fault Location in STATCOM-Integrated Transmission Networks Using Binary Quantum Walrus Optimizer (BQWO)
authors: Tejal Sanjay Barhate, S. A. Naveed
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Accurate fault location in STATCOM-integrated transmission networks remains a critical challenge due to nonlinear interactions between converter-based compensation and transient system dynamics. The present paper proposes a new Binary Quantum Walrus Optimizer (BQWO), developed to estimate optimal fault locations in such complex environments. The approach formulates fault distance and resistance estimation as a nonlinear optimization problem, which minimizes the differences between voltage measurements of two terminals. The quantum binary encoding improves global exploration, whereas walrus-inspired cooperative foraging achieves faster and more stable convergence. The optimizer is combined with Kernel Regression Residual and Improved Intrinsic Time-Scale Decomposition (K2RS2ITSD) preprocessing to remove distortions that STATCOM introduces in the voltage and current signals. The proposed approach is validated on an IEEE 9-bus system in MATLAB/Simulink under multiple fault types, locations, and resistances. The findings indicate that BQWO achieves fault location errors below 1 %, convergence time under 150 ms, and higher robustness compared with PSO and GA algorithms. The method provides an effective and scalable framework for highaccuracy fault localization in modern FACTS-compensated transmission systems.

title: CrimeSketch AI: Text-To-Image Synthesis for Criminal Identification Using Deep Learning
authors: Amrutha Sivakumar, Shreya Bhanot, Srinidhi M, Jyotsna C, Santhanalakshmi S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical, Communication, and Computing Technologies (iCONECCT)
abstract: Facial identification based on verbal eyewitness descriptions remains a significant challenge in contemporary forensics, particularly in the absence of visual evidence. Traditional methods such as CNN-based models and Haar cascade classifiers struggle with occlusion, poor lighting, and low-resolution inputs, while artist-drawn sketches are impractical for scalable automation. To overcome these limitations, we introduce CrimeSketch AI, a deep learning pipeline for generating realistic facial images from natural language descriptions. Our system combines Stable Diffusion fine-tuned by LoRA for text-to-sketch generation, Canny edge detection with ControlNet or CycleGAN for sketch-to-image translation, and StyleGAN2-ADA with CLIP for text-to-face synthesis. Identity matching is done using InsightFace with ArcFace embeddings to assure the accurate recognition. The model was tested on publicly released datasets, such as CelebA and a cleaned criminal mugshot dataset. CrimeSketch AI presents a scalable, privacy-preserving method for AI-powered suspect identification, augmenting the capability of law enforcers based on text-based descriptions alone.

title: Risks Associated with Cloud Computing and Data Security: A Comprehensive Analysis
authors: Sarasij Majumdar, Shivanath Ghosh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Cloud computing along with its foundational technology, modern information systems, facilitates dynamic access to shared computing infrastructure, on-demand computing, storage, and flexible delivery of services. Even though it is a widespread technology, cloud computing also carries many securities and substantively privacy challenges arising from its virtualization technologies, distributed architecture and multi-tenant environments. Among the foregoing challenges, data security reigns paramount over all, particularly with sensitive data of privacy at masses at disposal to the public and in hybrid cloud. The paper surveys and analyses security risks in cloud computing, particularly focusing on vulnerabilities to data security. As important risk factors, it takes note of virtualization exposure, environment exposure in public clouds, lack of robust authorization and access control, vendor lock-in issues, and risks associated with multi-tenancy and shared resource allocation. Security threats can be classified based on the various states of data, but this study gives the highest stress on vulnerabilities pertaining to data at rest and in transit-where threats such as unauthorized access to composite data, data leakage, interception, and compromise are manifested. Cryptographic defence mechanisms working in risk mitigation, e.g., symmetric key encryption: block ciphers and stream ciphers, and their existing plethora of drawbacks, adequacies, and effectuality towards either performance, scalability, and security requirements in cloud environments only further the contention. With such a background review and a juxtaposition of ongoing research and security practices, it is organized to present current challenges and actions to resolve issues concerning cloud data security, for bettering the design of secure and trustworthy cloud computing-systems, hopefully valuable to researchers, cloud service providers, and implementers.

title: National Data Centres and Sovereign AI Infrastructure: Designing Scalable Platforms for Jordan and Beyond
authors: Ahmad Al-Qerem
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: The adoption of large-scale artificial intelligence (AI) models has tightened the link between digital sovereignty and access to high-performance computing infrastructure. For emerging economies like the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan this poses both opportunity and risk. On the one hand, AI promises to transform sectors ranging from fintech and e-commerce to health and logistics, and Jordanian startups are already deploying chatbots, fraud detection and personalised recommendation systems to improve efficiency and customer experience. On the other hand, reliance on foreign hyperscale clouds can undermine data sovereignty, export capital and constrain national innovation. This paper delivers a data-grounded blueprint for a national data centre (NDC) and sovereign AI platform tailored to Jordan's economic and geopolitical context. Our design integrates detailed local metrics-such as the country's eight existing data centres (seven in Amman and one in Aqaba), electricity tariffs for large industry (0.17 USD <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\cdot \text{kWh}^{1}$</tex>), renewable energy contributing roughly <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$27-30 \%$</tex> of generation, the presence of 20 universities offering AI programmes to about 3000 students, and Jordan's commitment to train 15000 people and launch 25 government AI projects by 2027. We present a modular, pod-based architecture with secure sovereign enclaves, high-density AI pods and support for regional service delivery. A total cost of ownership (TCO) model grounded in Jordan-specific data shows that a 50 -rack phase-one facility would require roughly $ 25.7 M in capital expenditure and $ 3.5 M in annual operating expenditure, yielding a five-year TCO around 43.4 M; energy costs dominate long-term OPEX. We examine sensitivity to power-usage-effectiveness (PUE), electricity tariffs and GPU pricing. Finally, we map governance recommendations to existing national bodies defined in the Jordanian AI Strategy and illustrate the platform's impact through local startup case studies, including Repzo's AI-enabled CRM with more than 2,500 representatives and Abjjad's e-reading service with 1.5 million registered users.

title: Cryptocurrency Volatility Forecasting Using Garch, LSTM, Hybrid GARCH-LSTM and Hybrid CNN-LSTM with Intraday Frequency
authors: Houssemeddine Jlassi, Haykel Hamdi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Risk is a key concept in the world of finance. Measuring and predicting this critical aspect is therefore essential. Our paper compares three volatility prediction techniques: a traditional GARCH <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(1,1)$</tex> model, a machine learning model and two hybrid models: GARCH (1,1)-LSTM and CNN-LSTM. In contrast to the existing literature, our results show the invalidity of the hybrid models, which is outperformed by the traditional GARCH model and the LSTM model. This is explained by the slow reactivity and poor tail risk capture of these models. Thus, we note that the LSTM model is the most adequate and reliable for all subsamples.

title: Energy-efficient Silicon Nitride Optical Switching
authors: Yusen Huang, Jiaojiao Shi, Linye He, Xiaoyan Zhang, Perry Ping Shum, Feng Gao
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: The rapid scaling of artificial intelligence data centers driving an urgent demand for energy-efficient and reconfigurable optical switching technologies to overcome the bandwidth, latency, and power limitations of electronic interconnects. Silicon nitride (SiN) photonics offers ultra-low loss and CMOS compatibility but suffers from an intrinsically weak thermo-optic coefficient, leading to prohibitively high-power consumption in large-scale optical switch fabrics. To solve this, we propose a heterogeneous embedded-silicon (HES) design, where a thin silicon waveguide is integrated inside a low-loss SiN waveguide. This approach leverages the strong thermo-optic response of Si while preserving the excellent optical performance of SiN. Our optical switch based on a Mach-Zehnder interferometer (MZI) demonstrates a high peak extinction ratio of 44.7 dB at an ultralow electrical power of only 1.31 mW. This represents an 85% reduction in power consumption compared to conventional SiN optical switches with suspended design, achieving a near tenfold enhancement in modulation efficiency over state-of-the-art suspended SiN phase shifters. What's more, the entire structure is fabricated using a standard CMOS compatible process without exotic materials or post-processing, ensuring scalability and manufacturability. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first demonstration of a SiN optical switch with a sub-2-mW power consumption, establishing a new benchmark for energy-efficient photonic switching. Our approach directly addresses the power-efficiency bottleneck in large-scale optical interconnects and provides a practical pathway toward terabit-scale, low-energy photonic networks for next-generation AI accelerators.

title: A Systematic Literature Review on Cybersecurity Education: Trends, Challenges, and the Role of AI
authors: Yassine Mouhssine, Hicham Boutracheh, Amine Sadik, Aniss Moumen
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Cybersecurity education has become a crucial area of focus as cyber threats continue to evolve rapidly. This study presents a systematic literature review exploring the current trends, challenges, and advancements in cybersecurity education, with a particular emphasis on the role of artificial intelligence (AI) in cybersecurity competitions. To ensure a comprehensive analysis, we retrieved more than 500 articles from Scopus, of which over 298 were identified as directly relevant to our study. Capture The Flag (CTF) competitions, cyber ranges, and other hands on learning environments have emerged as essential tools for bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical cybersecurity skills. However, challenges such as scalability, accessibility, and real-time skill assessment persist. AI is increasingly being integrated into these competitions to enhance personalized learning, automate challenge generation, and provide intelligent feedback to participants. This review examines how AI-driven methodologies contribute to improving cybersecurity education by offering adaptive learning experiences, optimizing competition structures, and facilitating real-time performance evaluation. By synthesizing insights from recent research, this study identifies existing gaps and proposes future directions for leveraging AI in cybersecurity competitions to better equip students for real-world cyber defense scenarios.

title: Rest Allowance Models for Optimizing Task Scheduling and Worker Well-Being in Industry
authors: Dorsaf Aribi, Olfa Belkahla Driss, Hind Bril El Haouzi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Rest Allowance (RA) models have long served as essential tools for managing worker fatigue, optimizing task scheduling, and ensuring sustainable productivity in industrial environments. This paper provides a comprehensive review of existing RA models, encompassing traditional approaches based on standardized time allowances, ergonomic assessments, and more recent developments that account for dynamic fatiguerecovery patterns. We also identify key limitations that hinder their effectiveness in modern, human-centric manufacturing systems. These include the lack of personalization, limited integration of mental fatigue, and insufficient consideration of task sequencing and inter-worker dependencies. The paper concludes by highlighting the need for adaptive, personalized RA models that integrate physical and cognitive demands to enhance wellbeing and efficiency in reconfigurable production systems.

title: Scalable and Explainable Heart Disease Prediction on Imbalanced Data
authors: Mayssa Ben Fekih, Farah Jemili, Ouajdi Korbaa
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Heart disease remains a leading cause of mortality worldwide. With the increasing availability of digital health records, machine learning has emerged as a promising approach to facilitate early diagnosis. However, traditional deep learning methods often suffer from lengthy processing times, limited interpretability, and dependence on complex data formats, such as ECG signals. In this study, we present a practical and interpretable machine-learning framework for heart disease prediction. Our approach integrates Spark Streaming for scalable real-time data processing, LightGBM for efficient classification, SHAP for feature-level interpretability, and SMOTE to address class imbalance. We benchmark our method against recent deep learning and ensemble-based models. Experiments conducted on the large-scale Heart_2020_cleaned dataset from Kaggle demonstrate that our framework achieves high accuracy and AUC scores while maintaining fast execution and clear interpretability, making it a strong candidate for deployment in real- world clinical environments.

title:
High-Stability and High-Charge-Density HfZrO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    MFM Capacitors With In Situ CF
