authors: Dong-Ru Hsieh, Yi-Hsiu Chen, Li-Quan Yang, Chia-Chen Wan, Li-Ting Chou, Yi-Chen Chiang, Kuan-Lin Chen, Shang-Lin Hsieh, Yi-Sin Rao, Chun-Jung Su, Tien-Sheng Chao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: In this study, HfZrO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (HZO) metal–ferroelectric–metal (MFM) capacitors with in situ CF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub> plasma treatment at the bottom HZO/TiN interface were demonstrated and exhibited a sufficiently large two remnant polarization (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2{P}_{\text {r}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$26~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>C/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and significantly reduced <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2{P}_{\text {r}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> loss (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Delta 2{P}_{\text {r}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2{P}_{\text {r}, \text {pristine}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> from 52% down to 4% after the endurance test up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{{9}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cycles at 2 MV/cm since the metal oxide (TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> and TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>N<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {y}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at the TiN bottom electrode (BE) surface could be reduced by in situ CF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub> plasma treatment, and then, the formation of thin TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>N<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">y</sub> layer at the bottom HZO/TiN interface might be effectively suppressed by using a F-rich TiN BE during the thermal processes, therefore reducing the amount of oxygen vacancies (V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {o}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and repressing the formation of V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</sub>-rich HZO. This is beneficial to restrain the generation of new V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</sub> and then reduce the dipole pinning effect during field cycling, thereby enhancing the fatigue effect immunity. Using a CF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub> plasma-treated TiN BE with a rougher TiN surface to upgrade the charge density, the capacitance value is around doubled from 32 up to 61fF/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. From the ferroelectricity, charge storage capability, and reliability viewpoints, this approach is a promising candidate for embedded dynamic random access memories (eDRAMs).

title: An Intelligent Dimension-Reduction Method for Emergency-Supplier Evaluation Indicators Based on Filter-Wrapper and PSO-BP
authors: Jiayi Liu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Logistics Engineering, Supply Chain and Digital Transformation (LESCDT)
abstract: To address high-dimensional, redundant evaluation indicators and strong subjectivity in expert scoring within emergency-supply-chain contexts, an intelligent dimension-reduction model is proposed that integrates Filter-Wrapper feature selection with a PSO-BP neural network. An information-gain ratio is first employed for rapid preliminary screening, followed by hybrid recursive feature elimination that ensembles SVM, Random Forest and Gradient Boosting Machines for refined selection. Particle swarm optimization (PSO) is then applied to globally optimize the weights and thresholds of a BP neural network, yielding an adaptive evaluation model. Experiments on data from 93 suppliers indicate that the indicator dimensionality is compressed from 27 to 17 core features. With the PSO-BP approach, the model achieves a prediction accuracy of 94.6% and a recall of 93.3%, significantly outperforming SVM, Random Forest and the standard BP model. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of machine-learning techniques in supply-chain risk assessment and provide a reusable technical framework for indicator optimization in high-dimensional, small-sample scenarios.

title: Brown Bear Optimization-Based Error Minimization for Reduced-Order Modeling of Off-Grid Microgrids
authors: Richa Chaudhary, V. P. Singh, Akhilesh Mathur
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: Microgrid systems are the interconnection of various subsystems that consist of distributed energy resources, energy storage units, and loads that integrate to form complex higher-order systems (HOSs). However, assessing and controlling such complex HOSs shows significant challenges. To address these issues, reduced-order models (ROMs) are proposed to solve the HOS while conserving its essential system characteristics. In this paper, an approximation of the off-grid microgrid (OM) system is developed by employing the time moments (TMs) and Markov parameters (MPs). The proposed off-grid microgrid reduced-order model (OMROM) for the off-grid microgrid higher-order system (OMHOS) is obtained by minimizing the errors between the TMs and MPs of the OMHOS and its OMROM. For this error minimization, an objective function is developed and enhanced using the brown bear optimization (BBO) algorithm. The suggested OMROM is obtained under the criteria of the Hurwitz stability criterion and steady-state matching conditions. The effectiveness and applicability of the developed OMROM for representing the OMHOS are demonstrated through tabular analyses and graphical responses.

title: HAR70+W: Cross-Dataset Alignment of Thigh Back and Wrist Sensors for Elderly Activity Monitoring
authors: Abha Tewari, Dhananjay Kalbande
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: We present HAR70+W, a cross-dataset fusion approach for elderly activity monitoring that combines complementary inertial signals from two public sources: HAR70 + (thigh/back) sensors and WEDA-Fall wrist-worn signals. The proposed pipeline performs cross-dataset alignment and window-based segmentation to handle differences in sampling characteristics and recording protocols before learning activity representations. We benchmark several deep architectures, including 1D-CNN,LSTM,CNN+BiLSTM, Transformer, and a hybrid CNN+Transformer model. We introduce window based signal segmentation to enhance the performance of the recognition process. The model based on CNN+Transformer shows a considerable advantage over others at 90.62% accuracy and macro F1-score of 0.867. This framework is an attempt to overcome these problems, as shown by heterogeneous sampling rates and label alignment, and demonstrates that hybrid sensor fusion with advanced architectures has the potential to make a difference in practical elderly care use cases.

title: MIMO-Enabled Robust Communication Architecture for Fault-Tolerant Smart Grids Under Renewable Uncertainties
authors: Shubhalaxmi Mohapatra, Satyadeep Das, Debasis Nanda, Bibhu Prasad Sahoo, Sandeep Gupta, Kamalakanta Shaw, Priyanka Jethi, Subhendu Sekhar Sahoo
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The rapid growth of renewable energy generation and the increasing dependence on cyber-physical communication infrastructures have introduced new challenges to the reliability of modern smart grids. Renewable sources such as solar and wind exhibit stochastic variability that leads to rapid changes in power system operating conditions, resulting in electromagnetic interference, transient disturbances, and communication bottlenecks during critical events. Conventional single-input single-output (SISO) communication links often fail to maintain the latency, throughput, and outage performance required for protection, control, and wide-area monitoring. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a MIMO-enabled robust communication architecture designed specifically for smart grid environments subject to renewable intermittency and grid faults. The framework integrates multi-antenna transmission, adaptive switching between spatial diversity and spatial multiplexing, CSIaware real-time beamforming, and spatial interference suppression. A grid-event-driven adaptation mechanism is introduced, enabling the communication system to reconfigure transmission parameters based on fault severity, renewable ramp rates, and instantaneous channel conditions. Comprehensive simulations using MATLAB/Simulink demonstrate significant improvements in BER, SNR stability, outage probability, latency during protection events, and throughput during renewable ramp disturbances compared to SISO, OFDM, and fixed MIMO systems.

title: Emerging AI Methodologies in the Renewable Energy Ecosystem: Successes, Challenges, and Future Research Directions
authors: Temitope Bobola, Temitayo Fagbola
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming the renewable energy sector by enabling intelligent, adaptive, and data-driven solutions across key operational domains. This paper presents a comprehensive review of emerging AI methodologies applied to renewable energy forecasting, real-time grid coordination, energy management, predictive maintenance, and market optimization. Techniques such as machine learning (ML), deep learning (DL), and reinforcement learning (RL) have achieved notable gains in forecast accuracy, system reliability, distributed energy resource (DER) coordination, and economic efficiency. Despite these advancements, widespread adoption is hindered by data quality issues, legacy infrastructure, limited explainability, and cybersecurity risks. This review synthesizes current successes, identifies persistent challenges, and outlines future research priorities-including edge computing, transfer learning, human-in-the-loop systems, and regulatory frameworks-to support the scalable and secure deployment of AI in renewable energy systems. By addressing these barriers, AI can play a pivotal role in accelerating the transition to a resilient and net-zero energy future.

title: A Novel Deep Learning Framework for High-Accuracy Brain Tumor Diagnosis in Medical Imaging
authors: Sachin Chawla, Deepa Gupta, Sanjeev Kumar Pippal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Brain Tumors (BT) are abnormal clusters of neuronal cells that spread through all brain areas, and their presence can disrupt essential brain operations. Accurate assessment of BT is crucial because it enables timely treatment of this medical condition. Early BT assessment relies on established testing methods, which fail to deliver precise results because they depend on the abilities of the radiologist and the actual patient situation, and they encounter difficulties when assessing fragile tumor characteristics. The research developed a powerful Deep Learning (DL) framework that improves BT detection and classification from Magnetic Resonance Imaging scans. The proposed framework included a complete preprocessing system, which used brain stripping, bias field correction, z-score normalization, histogram equalization and data augmentation to achieve uniform image quality throughout all procedures. The attention net architecture performed tumor segmentation, while deep feature extraction was managed through a pre-accomplished efficient net model. The ensemble system employed Vision Transformer (ViT), XGBoost, and Support Vector Machine (SVM) models, which achieved optimal performance through hyperparameter tuning based on Bayesian Optimization. The framework achieved 99.85% accuracy during testing with the BraTS dataset, which surpassed multiple existing top methods.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Optimization Techniques For Transformer Based Rainfall–runoff Modeling
authors: Neha S. Chavhan, Pranay Saraf, Nekita Chavhan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: The increasing demand for accurate hydrological forecasting the modeling rainfall and runoff processes has become very important for water resource management and flood prevention. However, traditional physical models and recent neural architectures like ANN, CNN and LSTM struggle to capture long-term dependencies effectively. Transformer-based models use self-attention mechanisms that help to provide enhanced temporal feature learning and parallel computation capabilities. This paper presents a systematic review and simulated comparative analysis of Transformer optimizers, including Adam, RAdam, AdaBelief, Lookahead and Lion in rainfall-runoff forecasting. The study compares the convergence, generalization, and stability of optimizers, emphasizing AdaBelief and Lion for higher predictive stability and quicker convergence. The results offer important insights into the optimization strategies used in transformer-based hydrological models and also address the shortcomings of existing methods and offer ideas for future research, such as cross-catchment generalization and hybrid physical transformer models.

title: Cloud-Native Multimodal Semantic Search and Recommendation for Large-Scale Digital Commerce
authors: Amit Kumar Padhy, Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Vinay Soni, Arun Kumar Elengovan, Gajendra Babu Thokala, Nandagopal Seshagiri
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The explosive growth of large-scale digital commerce platforms has seen the need for intelligent product search systems that can comprehend textual and visual information. Traditional keyword-based retrieval methods and text only neural retrieval models may not be able to capture semantic intent, as well as visual similarity, resulting in less than optimal user experience and lower conversion rates. The purpose of current research effort is to design and test a cloud-native multi-model semantic search and recommendations framework to scale-text and image representations. The proposed methodology includes a combination of transformer-based multimodal encoders, distributed dense vector retrieval and microservices-based deployment orchestrated using Kubernetes. The system breaks down ingestion, embedding, indexing, retrieval, ranking and personalization processes into independently scalable services to deal with spiky e-commerce workloads. Extensive experiments on an actual product catalog show that the proposed framework has a higher retrieval effectiveness, gains up to 11.5 % in Precision@10 and 9.8% in nDCG@10 as compared to strong multimodal baselines, while saving almost <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 \%}$</tex> of p99 latency as compared to monolithic deployments. These results prove that cloud native multimodal retrieval is a practical and scalable solution for next generation digital commerce systems.

title: Educational and Career Outcome Forecasting Through Machine Learning Analytics
authors: Suha Khalil Assayed, Padmesh Tripathi, Pridhi Arora
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Understanding the factors that have impacts on salaries are more and more important in data-driven era. Factors such as education, experience, age, gender, and career choices play a key role in determining salaries. This study applies machine learning (ML) methods to explore the impact of these factors, providing insights that can guide employers and help students make informed career decisions. Multiple machine learning models were applied to predict employee salaries based on degree level, age, experience, gender, and career type. The dataset was obtained from a public source, data.world, and is derived from the US Adult Census data. Three ML algorithms were used: support vector machine (SVM), logistic regression (LR), and random forest (RF) classifier to predict whether salaries are above or below 50K. The models were then evaluated and tested using unseen data to assess their performance. The results exposed that LR classifier attained the highest accuracy in comparison to the other classifiers, whereas in terms of precision and F1-score, the RF performed better. In future work, neural network algorithms, including recurrent neural networks (RNNs) with activation functions like sigmoid and softmax, will be applied to classify predicted salaries as either above or below 50K.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Virtual Power Plants as an Enabler of Energy Future
authors: Sairam Challa, Ubaid Qureshi, Neha Tak
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The increasing integration of distributed energy resources (DERs) such as solar PV systems, wind turbines, battery storage, electric vehicles, and responsive demand technologies is significantly altering the architecture and operation of modern power systems. These decentralized components are disrupting the conventional hierarchical model of electricity generation and distribution, introducing complex challenges in demand-supply balancing, grid stability, and overall system efficiency. To address these challenges, the concept of Virtual Power Plants (VPPs) has gained prominence. VPPs aggregate diverse DERs into cohesive and controllable units, enabling flexible operation and market participation through sophisticated digital platforms. By coordinating various distributed assets, VPPs facilitate optimized scheduling and centralized or decentralized control. This paper presents an in-depth analysis of the core principles and current developments related to VPPs. It examines the primary architectural approaches highlighting their respective strengths and limitations regarding scalability, communication needs, interoperability, and system resilience. Furthermore, the paper discusses emerging technologies set to advance VPP functionality, including artificial intelligence for predictive control, blockchain for secure energy transactions, and digital twins for detailed simulation and system management.

title: Voltage and Frequency Stabilisation of A Deregulated Power System Using Multi-Stage Controller
authors: Subhadra Sahoo, Narendra Kumar Jena, Kumari Kasturi, Binod Kumar Sahu
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: In the present scenario, there is continuous increase in electricity demand in almost all sectors of industries. This huge load requirement is to be satisfied only by introduction of renewable energy sources. But the RES units due to lack of inertia and again due to uncertain inputs makes the system more unstable and complicated. Hence it becomes a great challenge to maintain the system stability of large interconnected power system in the presence of the RES units. Further to satisfy the consumers by providing reliable supply with less cost the systems are modified to a horizontally integrated system commonly known as deregulated system. In such complex system to maintain the voltage and frequency at a desired level, more reliable and efficient secondary controllers are to be designed. In this work, a two stage PID+1PI controller is designed for the dynamic stability of a two-area hydro-thermal-wind power system. Its result is compared with result of PID controller and proves its better performance. The controller parameters are selected through Selfish Herd Optimisation technique. The robustness of the controller is validated by inserting a random load.

title: Scalable Credit-Card Fraud Detection Using a RAAE and XGBoost
authors: G. Sindhu Sri, Ravuri Lalitha, Ch. Sai Viswanth, V. Lakshmi Sravya, S. Rehana Sulthana
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Credit-card fraud is a widespread worldwide issue that costs banks and customers billions annually and requires real-time identification so all transactions can be filtered through in seconds. Good modelling is difficult to accomplish because fraudulent activity is very rare in relation to valid transactions, fraud methods constantly change, and streams of transactions may contain noise or missing data. Traditional deep-learning approaches tend to overfit and are not transparent, which makes them too unreliable for deployment. This paper introduces Recency-Attentive Autoencoder with Extreme Gradient Boosting (RAAE+XGBoost) to solve these problems. The autoencoder learns the manifold of valid transactions and generates reconstruction-error features, whereas an attention mechanism weighs more heavily on recent and informative transactions. Other short-term statistical features maintain temporal trends. These enhanced features are input to an XGBoost classifier that can deal with extreme class imbalance and extract intricate nonlinear relationships. Model choices are justified by means of SHAP values to satisfy financial-sector interpretability standards. Stepwise ablation from a vanilla Autoencoder to AE + XGBoost and finally to full RAAE + XGBoost illustrates the way recency weighting, attention, and gradient boosting improve precision-recall performance stepwise. On the review-voted European credit-card dataset, the method achieves the highest precision-recall AUC and lowest false-negative rate among all the tested baselines in machine learning and deep learning, offering a accurate, scalable, and transparent framework for real-time fraud detection for banking and e-commerce settings.

title: Cost-Effective Reliability Analysis of RE Rich Grid with BESS by Horizon-Optimal Technique
authors: Prahlad Mundotiya, Arun Kumar Nayak, Santosh Kumari, Nirmala Yadav, Chandra Prakash Naga, N.K. Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The growing integration of renewable energy sources, particularly wind and photovoltaic systems, has transformed modern power system operations, introducing both environmental benefits and operational challenges due to their stochastic and weather-dependent nature. Accurate forecasting of renewable generation has thus become crucial for reliable and economical grid operation. This paper proposes a horizonoptimal linear programming framework that integrates probabilistic renewable forecasts directly into the battery energy storage system dispatch problem. Unlike deterministic or computationally intensive stochastic optimization methods, the proposed model incorporates multiple forecast scenarios within a rolling horizon, enabling adaptive, uncertainty-aware decision-making in real time. The approach jointly minimizes operating costs while maintaining system reliability and tractability. Simulation studies conducted on the IEEE 39-bus test system demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively balances economic performance and reliability under renewable uncertainty, outperforming traditional rule-based dispatch strategies. The results reveal that optimal BESS scheduling achieves up to a 13 % reduction in operational costs and a significant reduction in renewable curtailment compared to the no-storage scenario.

title: Smart Home Energy Management System Considering Battery Degradation Cost
authors: Vinay Kumar Yadav, Basant Kumar Sethi, Chandani P. Gor
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Since, smart home energy management systems (SHEMS) are becoming more popular, the homeowner has the opportunity to maximize energy utilization and make use of renewable resources effectively. However, the increased adoption of SHEMS leads to frequent battery charging and discharging, that effect life of the battery significantly. To deal with this issue, effective scheduling strategies must be considered for battery degradation costs along with homeowner requirement. The major objective of this method is to coordinate the operation of smart home appliances, energy resources, and the battery itself to achieve an overall reduction in household energy expenditure. Apart from it, the approach simultaneously integrates a model for battery degradation. The study rigorously investigates the effects of strategic, efficient demand scheduling on battery longevity and overall energy expenditures.

title: Technoeconomic Analysis and Smart Scheduling of Neighbourhood EMS Considering Shading Effect of PV and Ageing Effect of ESS
authors: Anchal, Sandeep Kakran, Ratna Dahiya
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: With the global rise in the integration of solar photovoltaics (PV) with the grid, consideration of energy storage systems (ESS) becomes important for efficient utilization of energy for the prolonged sustainability. ESS may include battery for storing and providing the energy generated from distributed energy resources (DERs). Consideration of degradation cost and life cycle of battery becomes an important aspect while including ESS in the system. This article includes solar PV, battery energy storage systems (BESS) and electric vehicles (EV) as DERs in the presence of peer-to-peer (P2P) energy exchange across the prosumers within the residential community. The research work further explores the impact of shading on the energy scheduling of a neighbourhood energy management system (NEMS) and also evaluates the performance of the BESS considering various parameters during the life cycle of battery. Battery capacity fade due to calendar-ageing and cyclicageing based degradation is calculated to estimate the end-of-life of the battery. This paper also analyses the payback period of PV coupled with battery system. The formulated problem is solved using SBB solver of General Algebraic Modelling Systems (GAMS) to optimize the cost function under Time of Use (TOU) demand response scheme. After optimization, 0.59 % decrement is obtained in the amount of cost of energy consumed by the community along with 9.1 % reduction in the amount of energy generated by solar PV due to consideration of shading effect in the system.

title: Simulation Study of Metal - Doped Graphene/Hexagonal Boron Nitride for Ammonia Sensing Application
authors: Ainun Khairiyah Taib, Zaharah Johari, Shaharin Fadzli Abd Rahman, Mohd Fairus Mohd Yusoff, Fatimah Khairiah Abd Hamid, Suhaila Isaak
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Sensors and Nanotechnology (SENNANO)
abstract: Graphene/Hexagonal Boron Nitride (hBN) has gained the attention of numerous researchers developing sensing devices due to its excellent physical and electrical properties. This study uses Density Functional Theory (DFT) simulation to investigate the effect of metal substitution on Graphene/hBN for adsorption of ammonia, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NH}_{3}$</tex> gas molecules. The energy band gap and density of state (DOS) are simulated to determine the changes in the conductivity. The charge transfer, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Q$</tex>, adsorption energy, E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ads</inf> and recovery time, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tau$</tex> are determined to evaluate the sensing interaction between the molecules and Graphene/hBN. Different types of metal, namely Platinum, Pt and Aurum, Au, are used to evaluate the gas adsorption behavior. Within the investigated simulation condition, results shows that metal doping affect the energy band gap. Most notably Pt and Au doped on Graphene/hBN significantly influence <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E}_{\text{ads.}}$</tex> A single <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P t}$</tex> doped improve <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E}_{\text{ads}}$</tex> from +0.9 eV (undoped) to −1.41 eV. An additional metal substituted exhibit slight reduction to −1.28 eV and −1.21 eV for bi - and tri - Pt doped. Single Au doped on Graphene/hBN shows very minimal adsorption (+0.02 eV). This is markably improves to - 1.46 eV and −1.59 eV for additional bi - and tri - Au doped. A single Pt - doped exhibits a recovery time that longer than Au doped likely due to stronger binding of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NH}_{3}$</tex> molecules on the Graphene/hBN surface. These findings provide insight in Graphene/hBN surface treatment approach for developing high performance sensor.

title: Design of Metamaterial MSPA for Wireless Applications
authors: Jayasukumari. I, Themozhi. G, Amali. C
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: A radiating patch on one side of the dielectric substrate and, consequently, the ground plane on the other side makes up a metamaterial coplanar waveguide patch antenna. Pure copper is utilised as the ground plane (conducting material) and as a patch, whereas FR-4 is used as the substrate (dielectric material). Several feeding methods are used in the design and simulation of the Metamaterial MSPA (Microstrip Patch Antenna). The frequency range in which the antenna operates is 3 to 6 gigahertz. The microwave studio of Computer Simulation Technology (CST) was used to create the high frequency Metamaterial coplanar waveguide patch antenna. In antenna design, the metamaterial is utilised to make the antenna smaller. In order to boost signal strength, which is crucial for long-range wireless networks, the antenna is examined for a number of criteria, including radiation pattern, gain, directivity, and efficiency, using microstrip line feed and coplanar waveguide feed.

title: Evaluating YOLOv12 and RT-DETR for Robust Brain Tumor Detection in MRI Scans
authors: Satyendra Yadav, Isha Saini, Vidushi Sharma, Rajiv Ratn Shah
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Accurate and quick detection of brain tumors from MRI scans is critical for its timely diagnosis and treatment planning. The evaluation of MRI images manually is strenuous and susceptible to variability among experts. Deep learning-based object detection models have turned up as promising solutions for automating tumor localization and classification. In this research we have compared two state-of-the-art architectures, YOLOv12 and RT-DETR, for the detection of brain tumors on two MRI datasets, containing multiple tumor classes. Both the models are trained and assessed under baseline and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times$</tex> augmented conditions. This assessment is based on metrics such as Precision, Recall, mAP@0.5, mAP@0.5:0.95, and Accuracy. Our experimental results proved that data augmentation remarkably raises model performance, improving both precision and generalization. YOLOv12 demonstrates higher overall precision, while the RTDETR shows great performance improvements on augmented dataset due to its design based on transformers. These findings further extend the importance of augmentation within medical imaging and validate YOLOv12 as a strong, efficient solution toward the automated detection of brain tumors in MRI scans.

title: Hybrid Force and Torque Control of a Four-Finger Robotic Gripper Using Pid and Tactile Feedback in Simulink Environment
authors: Azam Ameen, Mishkat Nizami, Mohd. Suhaib
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a four-fingered robotic gripper with hybrid force/torque control using MATLAB Simulink and Simscape Multibody. There are three revolute joints in each finger, thus the 12-DOF system is tendon-driven by PID controllers. In virtual tactile sensing, the spatial actuating force blocks can be used for real-time imitating. Position and force-controlled modes are switchable dynamically by the finite state machine implemented inside Stateflow. The system adapts the gripping of five object geometries- sphere, cube, cylinder, disc and ellipse. Simulations reveal true force control (5N ± 0.2 N), small angle error (<3°), and good torque control (~ 0.25 Nm). These observations revealed the effectiveness of the system to model “human-like dexterity” and thus “intelligent robotic manipulation in unstructured scenarios” can be realized through the system.

title: Landslide Detection and Warning System Using NB-IoT
authors: Muralidharan Perumal
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: This project describes about Landslide detection and warning system using IoT sensors, Narrow Band - IoT (NB-IoT) and Wireless Sensor Networks (WSN). NB-IoT are deployed along with BLE (BlueTooth Low Energy) with STAR Topology of sensore nodes. This project encompasses possibilities of early warning system, installtsion of monitoring system, setting up of appropriate alarm levels and alerting system to public and Govt authorities like District Administration (Ex: District Collector), Natural Disaster Management Authorities. Each node is deployed with distance between them as 20 mts. These sensor nodes will communicate via BLE 5 (Nordic nRF 52840 device) with Ultrs low power for communication. Among multiple sensors, one will be the master/gateway (Nordic nRF 9160) which will communicate with cloud and pass necessary crucial data like GPS, sensor data, node conectivity info etc. Also it will monitor and communicate with slave device's connectivity status to central control room. In the event of landslide event, based on the expert judgement, the possibility of landslide is hinted and alerting sytem sends alerts to public and govt authorities. Visible danger light and sound like siren alerts are provided in nearby roads to alert passing vehicles. Considering alert system, the nearby living public are alerted via SMS. Government authorities like District/Village administration, Natural disaster management authorities are alerted via email.

title: Harnessing Machine Learning and Deep Learning to Predict Cumulative Abnormal Returns from CEO Behavioral Profiles
authors: Said Benlakhdar, Mohammed Rziza, Rachid Oulad Haj Thami
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: Mergers and acquisitions (M&A) are central to corporate growth, yet post-acquisition performance frequently shows declining cumulative abnormal returns (CAR). This study examines how CEO behavioral traits, particularly hubris and learning ability, shape CAR outcomes across 7,786 international M&A transactions conducted between 2012 and 2022. We assess the extent to which successive deal experience, deal completion, and time between deals interact with CEO characteristics to influence acquisition performance. Using econometric analysis enriched with machine learning methods and deep learning techniques, we capture both linear and non-linear patterns linking CEO traits to post-acquisition value creation. Although CEO characteristics account for a modest share of CAR variance, they exert significant effects on deal timing, success likelihood, and value outcomes. Hubristic CEOs display more aggressive acquisition behavior, while learning-oriented CEOs show improved performance over successive deals. These findings advance the understanding of the behavioral drivers of M&A performance and highlight the value of integrating executive behavioral assessment with data-driven predictive tools in strategic decision making.

title: Design and Development of a Mobile Application for Homework Supervision and Management
authors: Mritha Ramalingam, R. Puviarasi, Elanchezhian Chinnavan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: There is a growing trend of diminished motivation among school students to complete homework assignments, as they frequently devise methods to circumvent these responsibilities. Educators encounter difficulties in overseeing students' activities outside the classroom, and there is a prevailing belief that parents may not enforce strict homework guidelines. Additionally, students often resort to providing excuses or misleading their parents about the status of their assignments. This research introduces a strategy to enhance communication between educators and parents, thereby minimizing students' opportunities to neglect homework obligations. The central objective of this study is to create a mobile application that records and disseminates information regarding homework assignments, allowing both educators and parents to effectively track students' progress. The application is designed with intuitive interfaces to ensure user accessibility. Considering the extensive use of smartphones, a mobile platform was chosen for the development of this application.

title: A Real-Time Audio-Visual System for Indian Currency Authentication Using Probabilistic Neural Networks in Python
authors: B. Ravi Chandra, P Akshay Babu, P Abdul Subhan, R Dilip Kumar, Ajay Roy
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Fake money continues to be a significant economic risk in the modern digital age. The demand for a low-cost software-driven solution is made clear by the high cost and inaccessibility of conventional hardwarebased detectors. This article presents a real-time audiovisual Indian currency authentication system that makes use of a probabilistic neural network (PNN) that is written in Python. The model makes use of hybrid feature extraction, including color histograms, Sobel, LBP, and HOG, along with CLAHE for light normalization. A trained PNN uses live video from an IP camera to classify notes worth between ₹10 and ₹2000, giving immediate vocal input. The experimental findings demonstrate great precision, inexpensive computation, and excellent applicability for real-time application in banking and retail settings.

title: AI-Driven Hybrid Framework for Lung Cancer Detection Using Deep and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Shilpa Sharma, Vijay Mohan Shrimal, Shubham Sharma, Tapasya Sharma, Girish Paliwal, Kanta Prasad Sharma, Madani Hamdi Mohammed Rashed
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Globally, lung cancer continues to rank among the leading causes of mortality. The lack of sophisticated technology choices for screening and the fact that the sickness is typically discovered at the final stage are the main causes of this. Consequently, this work creates a hybrid AI-based system that combines machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) methods to make lung cancer detection quick and accurate. A machine learning classifier such as Random Forest or Support Vector Machine (SVM) is used for the final diagnosis after the created system uses convolutional neural networks (CNN) for automatic feature extraction from computed tomography (CT) images. Such a hybridization allows the model to leverage the deep networks' superior feature extraction ability and also, maintain the interpretability and the efficiency of traditional ML algorithms. The proposed model through rigorous experiments on benchmark datasets is reported to achieve classification accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity of 97.8%, 96.5%, and 98.2%, respectively, thus, outperforming the conventional standalone methods. Besides, the designed scheme is perfect in differentiating malignant and benign lung nodules under various imaging conditions.

title: Psycho-Intelligent Dialogue Agents for Enhancing Emotional Self-Regulation in Autistic Teenagers
authors: Celestine Iwendi, Negin Aboutorabi, Adedeji Edward Adesola, Collins Lemeke, Gladys Chisom Okoro, Vandana Sharma
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Autistic adolescents often experience the inability to identify their emotions and self-regulate them, thus creating the impulse for the construction of intelligent assistive technologies. Building on this premise, this work proposes a novel Psycho- Intelligent Dialogue Agent (PIDA) system, which attempts to incorporate advances in affective computing, contextualised rea-soning, and psychotherapeutic dialogues, in aiding emotional self- regulation with teenagers with autism spectrum disorder (ASD). This system integrates a visual emotion recognition model with an adaptive conversational bow. To train the emotion classifier for real time application trained using transfer learning techniques based on the VGG16 architecture of deep convolutional neural networks, it was trained on a specialised dataset comprising of autistic children's facial expressions and achieved an accuracy of 71% at a 5-emotion recognition task. The Effect recognition module serves the context-aware dialogue manager in real time adapting and personalising the emotional regulation frameworks to be employed. PIDA's dialogues are based on the principles of clinical psychotherapy, with psychotherapeutic techniques and intervention strategies which are individually tuned to the emotional state and contextual parameters of the situation. The system was designed and built salted with caregiver integration features to enable guardians to monitor progress and active participant in the personalization of the intervention. Primary experimental results reflect the feasibility of this dimension in emotional awareness and emotional regulation and coping strate- gies. To support we provide uninterrupted emotional assistance to autistic young people and offer flexible support resources during and in between emotional therapy appointments.

title: ML Forecasting of Tunisia's Regulatory Freedom Index
authors: Amani Driss, Yamina Mathlouthi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: This article explains how machine learning techniques can be used to forecast the ‘Regulation’ component (Area 5) of the economic freedom index published by the Fraser Institute for the next five years (2023-2027) for Tunisia. This component examines the effect of government regulations on voluntary trading in the credit, laborbor and business markets. The study demonstrates Tunisia's institutional inertia through the Ridge regression, Random forest regression, and XGBoost regression methods. The results were compared to those of simple univariate forecasting methods such as the naive, average, and drift models used in previous studies. Institutional theories underpin the results, which highlight persistent challenges and suggest improvements to the regulatory framework.

title: Fuzzy PID Control Method for Finished Cigarette Packaging Robots
authors: Chenle Liu, Shoufeng Jin, Jie Zan, Ziran Zhang, Dian Pan, Linjiang Li
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the issue of low precision in the motion trajectories of robots during the packaging process of finished cigarettes, this paper proposes a control method for robot packaging using a fuzzy PID controller. By analyzing the packaging process of finished cigarettes, a fuzzy PID control model for the robot is established. The fuzzy PID controller is designed based on the robot's D-H parameters, kinematic, and dynamic equations. Fuzzy rules are designed to dynamically adjust the PID parameters, and a simulation experiment is conducted to analyze the robot's trajectory tracking using a sine wave test signal. The experimental results indicate that the proposed method can improve the accuracy of the robot's trajectory tracking. Error analysis shows that the tracking error is reduced by an average of 97.47%. Under external interference, the absolute error of the robot's joint angles is controlled within 1.96°.

title: Research and Application of Online Detection Technology in the Inspection of Adsorption Towers in PSA Devices
authors: Dapeng Wang, Xiushan Shi, Pengcheng Gan, He Yan
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: The adsorption tower plays a crucial role in the normal operation of the PSA unit as the core equipment. Due to the special structure of the adsorption tower and the particularity of the adsorbent, it is generally only conduct in-material detection externally. In this paper, the author identified its damage pattern based on the working conditions of the adsorption tower. On this basis, the online detection method of the system has been proposed, then conducts targeted detection on the defect types and defect locations that are prone to occur in this equipment. The results show that the proposed damage mode and online detection method can effectively identify harmful defects and provide targeted treatment suggestions, offering reference and guidance for the safe and stable operation of the adsorption tower.

title: Segment-Based Advanced Image Encryption Scheme for Secure Image Transmission
authors: Jaishree Jain, Saroj Kushwah, Updesh Kumar Jaiswal, Anuradha Dhumale, Mani Dublish, Aastha Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Sciences and Communications (ICCSC)
abstract: The necessity to safeguard picture data from unwanted access has grown due to the quick development of digital media, especially in industries like online content distribution, military communication, and medical imaging. In this paper a new approach toward image encryption and decryption for attaining increased security in both storage and transmission. Our approach breaks down the images into segments before the process of encryption application on the images increases complexity and strength of the encryption process. Every segment is encrypted separately by using a 16 character key, which is supposed to be provided by the user. Thus, ensuring that any partial decryption attempt would be futile without an accurate key. Our approach is very simple and user friendly, without compromising on security. This approach uses a web application interface developed using HTML and JAVA SCRIPT for user interaction like uploading images. This work demonstrates a Python implementation for the encryption system that uses high modularity and scalability. Advanced segmentation- based encryption guards sensitive image data from unintended access. The technique, in particular, is great for secure image transfer. It is used to privately store confidential data. More importantly, this application technique provides a practical solution for modern cybersecurity issues.

title: Capacity Analysis and Robust Topology Design of LEO Satellite Constellation with Node/Link Failure
authors: Lei Guo, Junyu Liu, Min Sheng, Yang Li, Jiandong Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite networks are increasingly central to wide-area communication services, yet their capacity is highly sensitive to the inherent vulnerability of satellite and inter-satellite link (ISL) failures. This paper develops an analytical framework for characterizing network capacity under arbitrary failure patterns. Building on bisection cut-set analysis, we formalize capacity bottlenecks through graph partitioning and derive rigorous upper bounds on the network capacity. Within this framework, we further identify and characterize the maximum failure cut set, i.e., a critical subset of ISLs whose removal yields the most severe capacity degradation, and establish its necessity in achieving the worst-case capacity bound. Furthermore, the analytical framework has been shown to be applicable to general LEO networks, and a closed-form expression for capacity degradation has been derived using a 2D torus topology as an example, which takes into account both the number and spatial distribution of the satellite or ISL failure. The results indicate that maximizing the minimum bisection cut set capacity is fundamental to strengthening the intrinsic robustness of the LEO network. This paper provides a theoretical methodology for capacity assessment and offers principled guidelines for topology design in satellite constellations.

title: Combining I-BSIF Fingerprints and FlexenTech for Reversible Medical Image Watermarking
authors: Brahim Ferik, Lakhdar Laimeche, Abdallah Meraoumia, Abdelkader Laouid, Khaled Chait
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: The rise of digital medical imaging in telemedicine raises concerns about image authenticity and patient privacy. Traditional watermarking methods struggle to achieve robust security and reliable patient authentication simultaneously. This study presents a novel blind reversible watermarking framework that combines orientation-guided Improved Binary Statistical Image Features (I-BSIF) with FlexenTech encryption to improve medical image security. The method utilizes advanced fingerprint feature extraction tailored to local ridge orientations, resulting in compact biometric signatures. These features undergo lightweight FlexenTech encryption before being embedded into medical images through spread-spectrum modulation in mid-frequency subbands of the Lifting Wavelet Transform. Experimental results demonstrate that rotation-invariant feature extraction preserves more than 99% similarity after a 270° rotation, with an average Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) exceeding 51 dB and a Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) of nearly 0.999, confirming the method's effectiveness in maintaining patient authentication and image integrity.

title: A Data-Driven Optimal Control Architecture for Grid-Connected Power Converters
authors: Ruohan Leng, Linbin Huang, Huanhai Xin, Ping Ju, Xiongfei Wang, Eduardo Prieto-Araujo, Florian Dörfler
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: Grid-connected power converters are ubiquitous in modern power systems, acting as grid interfaces of renewable energy sources, energy storage systems, electric vehicles, high-voltage DC systems, etc. Conventionally, power converters use multiple PID regulators to achieve different control objectives such as grid synchronization and voltage/power regulation, where the PID parameters are usually tuned based on a presumed (and often overly-simplified) power grid model. However, this may lead to inferior performance or even instabilities in practice, as the real power grid is highly complex, variable, and generally unknown. To tackle this problem, we employ a data-enabled predictive control (DeePC) to perform data-driven, optimal, robust, and adaptive control for power converters. We call the converters that are operated in this way DeePConverters. A DeePConverter can implicitly perceive the characteristics of the power grid from measured data and adjust its control strategy to achieve optimal, robust, and adaptive performance. We present the modular configurations, generalized structure, control behavior specification, inherent robustness, detailed implementation, computational aspects, and online adaptation of DeePConverters. High-fidelity simulations and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) tests are provided to validate the effectiveness of DeePConverters.

title: Adaptive MPC-based Path Following Control for Autonomous Vehicles with Random Time Delays
authors: Chao Yang, Ruixin Zhang, Weida Wang, Yuhang Zhang, Qi Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Autonomous vehicles are further improved with the development of in-vehicle networks and X-by-wire technologies. Due to the increasing integration of vehicle systems and the growing complexity of control links, time delays in information transmission and execution have become increasingly prominent. Because of late responses to control inputs, autonomous vehicles have deterioration in terms of path following accuracy and control stability, which poses a threat to driving safety. To solve this problem, an adaptive model predictive path following control strategy is presented in this paper. Firstly, a real-time delay estimator is designed based on historical feedback information processing and the gradient descent algorithm. Secondly, based on the estimated delay, a delay-augmented model with variable dimension is proposed for adaptive control to mitigate the impact of time-varying delay. Meanwhile, a tube-based model predictive control method is modified for the proposed model to deal with uncertainties generated by time-varying delays. Finally, the proposed control strategy is verified by simulations and experiments in multiple cases. The results show that the proposed strategy achieved a smaller lateral offset than that in the ordinary model predictive control method, leading to a 52.86% improvement in accuracy under the existence of time-varying delays.

title: Smart Power Management in PV Systems with Battery–Supercapacitor Hybrid Storage for Electric Vehicle
authors: Dounia Touahria, Amel Bouchemha, Sami Kahla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: This study suggests a system of hybrid energy storage (HESS) for use alone photovoltaic (PV) applications. The system integrates a lithium-ion battery and a supercapacitor (SC) to ensure both long-term energy supply and fast dynamic response to power fluctuations. While the battery provides sustained energy delivery, its lifetime is negatively affected by frequent high-current demands. To mitigate this issue, the SC is introduced to absorb rapid transient loads, thereby reducing stress on the battery and extending its service life. The PV system charges both storage elements when solar irradiance is sufficient and ensures energy availability during low irradiance conditions. To highlight the benefit of the SC, a baseline configuration using PV with battery-only storage is also simulated. Power distribution across the storage devices is coordinated using a Genetic Algorithm (GA)-optimized energy management method based on proportional-integral (PI) controllers. MATLAB/Simulink results under varying irradiance and load profiles demonstrate that, compared to the baseline, the proposed HESS reduces battery SOC fluctuation, improves DC-bus voltage stability, and enhances overall energy utilization efficiency.

title: Attacker-Aware Contrastive Learning for Robust Malicious URL Classification
authors: Sara Afzal, Abraham Gebrehiwot, Farrukh Aslam Khan, Filippo Maria Lauria
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Uniform Resource Locators (URLs) are frequently exploited as entry points for cyberattacks such as phishing, malware distribution, and website defacement. Detecting malicious URLs directly from their lexical structure, without page retrieval, is essential for low-latency defense but remains challenging due to adversarial obfuscations (e.g., homoglyphs, zero-width insertions, subdomain stuffing) and severe class imbalance in real-world corpora. To address these issues, we propose an attacker-aware lexical classification pipeline that combines structure-preserving tokenization, a library of attacker-style augmentations, supervised contrastive (SupCon) pretraining, and class-weighted focal loss fine-tuning. The Transformer encoder serves as the feature extraction model, while a lightweight MLP head performs classification. Empirical evaluations performed on the large Kaggle malicious phishing dataset show that the proposed methodology achieves the accuracy of 99.57% and the macro-F1 score of 99.38% on a hold-out test set, and thus consistently outperforms previously established state-of-the-art baselines. Notably, minority classes, which includes malware, achieves an accuracy and macro-G1 score greater than 99% in all aspects of measurement, highlighting the resentment of the method to adversarial string perturbations. The framework developed is computationally efficient, content agnostic and capable of deployment in a real-time operational scenario that provides a pragmatic defence mechanism against modern cyber threats.

title: Investigating the Impact of Medical Knowledge on Self-Medication Practices Among Non-Medical Students
authors: Saumendra Ku. Mohanty, Raghu N, Ansh Kataria, V.J.K. Kishore Sonti, Kunal Meher, Varalakshmi Dandu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Self-medication among non-medical students has become a prevalent global issue, often influenced by varying levels of medical knowledge. Understanding the relationship between knowledge and self-medication behaviours is essential for minimizing associated health risks. Existing studies primarily rely on descriptive surveys, which fail to capture nuanced risk patterns and knowledge-behaviour associations. To address these limitations, this study introduces the Knowledge-Behavior Impact Model (KBIM), integrating KMeans Clustering for grouping students by knowledge-practice profiles and Chi-Square Analysis for testing statistical associations in risk classification. The proposed model enables the accurate identification of high-risk self-medication behaviors based on objective knowledge levels and behavioral indicators. Findings reveal that students with lower medical knowledge scores exhibit significantly riskier self-medication practices <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5 \%}$</tex> for antibiotics, while those with higher knowledge demonstrate safer and more informed behaviours. The KBIM framework thus provides a robust analytical basis for designing targeted educational interventions in nonmedical academic settings.

title: Small Spot Size and High Transmission Cold Cathode Electron Gun Based on Sharp Point Tungsten Emitter With Suppressor Electrode
authors: Wenrui Liang, Shuai Tang, Junhao Zhong, Caijian Zhang, Yan Shen, Yu Zhang, Shaozhi Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Cold cathode electron guns have demonstrated significant advantages of high resolution and high brightness in vacuum electronic devices. However, the tradeoff between electron transmittance and focal spot size (FSS) is a big challenge. This article proposes a scheme of using a sharp cathode and a suppressor electrode to achieve a small FSS and high transmittance in a cold cathode electron gun simultaneously. Through initial finite-element simulations of an electron beam, the electron gun with a sharp cathode shows much localized electric field and induces an original convergent electron beam. The electron gun with suppressor electrode reduces the electric field gradient on the cathode surface and generates a much focused beam combined with the gate electrode. The influence of the suppressor electrode and the cathode curvature radius on the electron transmittance and electron FSS is measured. The results show that after introducing the suppressor electrode structure and using a cathode with a smaller radius of curvature, the electron beam FSS has decreased by 53.8%, and the electron transmittance has increased by 12.2%. At present, the electron gun with a sharp tungsten field emitter of tip radius of 10 nm and suppressor electrode shows an electron beam FSS of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$88~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m and an electron transmittance of 42.7% under an acceleration voltage of 20 kV. The results provide a feasible solution for achieving high electron transmission and small FSS of cold cathode guns.

title: Unveiling Volatility Drivers: Machine Learning and SHAP Across Weak and Strong Institutions
authors: Mohamed Malek Belhoula, Ichrak Dridi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Leveraging eleven machine learning models in a cross-country analysis, this study investigates the drivers of stock market volatility, emphasizing institutional quality's (IQ) pivotal role across high- and low-quality regimes in emerging markets. Employing regularization techniques (Lasso, Ridge, Elastic Net), tree-based models (Decision Tree, Extra Tree, Random Forest, LightGBM, XGBoost), and deep learning approaches (LSTM, ANN, GRU), alongside volatility models (EGARCH, HAR-GARCH, GARCH-MIDAS), we analyze 16 emerging countries with stable policy frameworks, over 1995Q1–2024Q1. Both mean SHAP feature importance and SHAP interaction techniques reveals distinct volatility dynamics: in high institutional quality environments, volatility is shaped by long-term market fundamentals and stable global financial linkages. In contrast, in low institutional quality settings, volatility is driven by immediate economic fragilities, speculative trading, and external uncertainties. Robust across tranquil and crisis periods, these findings affirm institutional quality's moderating role as a critical determinant of financial market stability. These insights offer significant implications for risk management and policy formulation in emerging economies.

title: Robust UAV Deployment and Task Offloading Based on Road Traffic Flow in Low-Altitude Wireless Networks
authors: Weifeng Zhong, Jiahai Xiao, Xumin Huang, Jiawen Kang, Shengli Xie, Chau Yuen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper studies long-term decision-making for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) deployment and task offloading in a UAV-enabled low-altitude wireless network (LAWN), which offers computing service to moving vehicles on a road segment. A key challenge in such high-mobility scenarios is that real-time decision-making demands frequent updates, leading to considerable implementation costs. Hence, our objective is to derive steadfast decisions that remain valid throughout system operation and robust against vehicle mobility under certain traffic flow, thereby eliminating the need for repeated decision updates. We formulate a data-driven joint UAV deployment and task offloading optimization model, where incorporating richer historical vehicle data yields more robust decisions. The optimization aims to minimize deployed UAVs' energy consumption, resulting in a nonconvex mixed-integer problem. To solve the problem efficiently, we develop a tailored heuristic that integrates the alternating direction method (ADM) and analytical target cascading (ATC). Results demonstrate that the proposed heuristic can adapt UAV placement and offloading strategies to specific traffic conditions and produce decisions that are robust to on-road vehicle dynamics.

title: Optimization of Machine Equipment Fault Prediction Model Based on Ensemble Learning
authors: Jiaquan Dai, Jianzhi Chen, Gongxing Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: This paper is based on the design and development of models using ensemble learning for fault prediction in mechanical equipment. It addresses the issues of imbalanced equipment datasets and the scarcity of data for faulty machines by oversampling the fault data to increase the amount of data for malfunctioning machines. By incorporating a cost function, the model introduces probability estimation and misclassification costs in the classification process, reclassifying the outcomes. Finally, the model is evaluated using ROC curves and confusion matrix metrics to reduce classification errors. The established model provides reliable support for the timeliness of machine maintenance.

title: Application of Ultra-Wideband to a Cut-to-Length Forest Harvester for Decimeter-Level Positioning Under Forest Canopies
authors: Zuoya Liu, Heikki Hyyti, Harri Kaartinen, Tamás Faitli, Matias Mäki-Leppilampi, Antero Kukko, Juha Hyyppä
publish: 2025 International Ubiquitous Positioning, Indoor Navigation and Location-Based Services Conference (UPINLBS)
abstract: In this paper, we present an ultra-wideband (UWB)based method for locating a cut-to-length forest harvester (CTL-FH) under forest canopies with decimeter-level accuracy. The proposed method involves placing and fixing several UWB beacons around the area of operation and one UWB receiver on the top of the harvester. This work is the first in the literature to integrate UWB positioning into a CTL-FH, which achieves decimeter-level accuracy, addresses the unavailability of Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) in positioning the forest harvester under forest canopies, and provides a real-time positioning solution for forest machines. The experimental results show that the proposed method can accurately and continuously locate the forest harvester under forest canopies with a root-mean-square error (RMSE) of 0.28 cm, providing a strong foundation and reference for the future applications of forest harvesters, such as individual tree-based harvest plan.

title: A Neural Algorithm Approach to Turbine Floor Noise Prediction Based on TabPFN
authors: Haitian Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechatronics, Automation and Intelligent Control (MAIC)
abstract: Accurate prediction of noise signals is of great significance for evaluating the operation of hydroelectric generating units. This study conducted a comparative analysis using the Random Forest (RF) and Tabular Prior-data Fitted Network (TabPFN) algorithms, and established a turbine floor noise regression model based on rotational speed, guide vane opening, and active power. The results show that both models can describe the overall changing trend of noise. However, RF has problems such as response lag, overreaction and low stability of multiple operation results when facing rapidly changing working conditions. In contrast, TabPFN has demonstrated its advantages in terms of fitting accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}=0.994$</tex>), tracking ability, and stability in multiple modeling, showing its potential in the noise prediction task of hydroelectric generating units. In addition, sensitivity tests show that TabPFN has significantly stronger robustness against input disturbances. When the operating condition variables are subject to small random disturbances, its prediction results can still remain stable. However, as a neural model, TabPFN has insufficient interpretability. Future research may consider integrating physical principles or explainable learning methods into models to enhance their interpretability.

title: Deepfake Detection: A Hybrid Deep Learning Approach Using ResNext and LSTM Models
authors: Md Shohel Arman, Md Asaduzzaman, Touhid Bhuiyan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: Deepfake technology, leveraging advancements in deep learning, has become a significant threat to digital media authenticity, enabling the creation of hyper-realistic yet deceptive videos that challenge existing detection methods. This paper presents a hybrid approach combining ResNext Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for frame-level feature extraction and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for analyzing temporal dependencies to improve deepfake detection accuracy. The study utilized a balanced dataset comprising videos from FaceForensic++, Celeb-DF, and custom-crafted deepfakes, with preprocessing steps that included facial region cropping, frame standardization, and noise reduction. The proposed model achieved an accuracy of 94.87 %, outperforming existing methods by effectively capturing both static and dynamic video features. Key innovations include leveraging the complementary strengths of CNNs and LSTMs to address frame-level and sequential inconsistencies in fake media. This approach is validated through extensive experimentation, demonstrating robustness against evolving generative adversarial techniques. The results establish a strong foundation for scalable and real-time detection applications, with future work aiming to enhance detection for multi-modal data and improve computational efficiency for deployment in resource-constrained environments.

title: YOLO-RDFN: Steel Surface Defect Detection Model
authors: Baolong You, Li Min, Pengyang Song, Yadong Hao, Yuhan Xu, Xinna You, Shizhe Zheng
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: To address the issues of complex structure, large parameter count, difficulty in detecting multi-scale targets, and poor performance in dense scenarios found in current steel surface defect detection models, this paper proposes a lightweight detection model based on YOLOV11. Firstly, the backbone network of YOLOV11 is replaced with RepGhostNet to achieve lightweight design and improve detection speed. Secondly, an RDFPN model is designed and embedded with the multi-scale dense object enhancement attention mechanism, MdAM. Finally, a multi-scale perception loss function based on distance, DWLIOSS, is designed to enhance detection performance in dense scenes. To further balance model lightweighting and detection accuracy, the modified network model is pruned to trim redundant parameters and compress the model further. Experimental results demonstrate that compared to the original YOLOV11 model, the improved model achieves a 9.89% increase in MAP, a 25.88% reduction in parameters, and a 17.68% improvement in detection speed.

title: Navigating Artificial Intelligence Adoption Decisions in Entrepreneurial Ventures: Insights from the Extended Technology-Organization-Environment Framework
authors: K.K. Shihab, Arif Hasan, Shubhangi Vitthal Gaikwad, Mini Agrawal, Sonal Sharma, Sandeep Raghuwanshi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: This study investigates factors that influence how entrepreneurs in India are adopting artificial intelligence. 462 entrepreneurs belonging to different industries such as service, trade and manufacturing sectors were approached. Exploratory factor analysis-(EFA), confirmatory factor analysis-(CFA) along with structural equation modeling-(SEM) techniques were used for data analysis through SPSS (v22) and AMOS (v24). The results show that the organizational preparedness, technological commitment and external pressure play a major role in driving AI adoption whereas, sub-factors perceived ease of use and usefulness have minimal influence. These findings suggest that entrepreneurs need to pay more attention to organization and external market dynamics, rather than simple technological interfaces. This study also contributes to the challenges faced in AI adoption, especially by extending and validating the TOE-TAM framework within an entrepreneurial set-up. These insights can really help policymakers concerning challenges entrepreneurs faced with AI.

title: Enhancing FANET Security with Elliptic Curve Cryptography: Protocol Evaluation and Analysis
authors: Sandeep Rai, Anoj Kumar, J.Sathish Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) operating in Flying Ad-hoc Networks (FANETs) are becoming essential for applications like surveillance, agricultural monitoring, disaster relief, and military missions due to their fast deployment and autonomous functionality. However, FANETs' dynamic nature, constant movement, and open wireless communications introduce critical security vulnerabilities, making them targets for threats such as data interception, identity spoofing, message alteration, and routing attacks. This study proposes a secure communication approach using Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC), a resource-efficient public-key encryption technique ideal for UAVs with limited processing capabilities. The ECCbased security protocol is incorporated into three common routing algorithms—4Ad-hoc On-demand Distance Vector (AODV), Optimized Link State Routing (OLSR), and Destination-Sequenced Distance Vector (DSDV)—which represent different routing paradigms: reactive, proactive, and table-driven. Simulation tests under realistic FANET scenarios, including varying node densities and mobility patterns as well as active security attacks like black hole, Sybil, and man-in-themiddle, assessed metrics such as packet delivery rate, latency, throughput, and control overhead. ECC-enhanced AODV offers the optimum balance between security and performance, according to research showing that incorporating ECC greatly improves security while maintaining network efficiency. This study shows that integrating cryptographic techniques at the routing layer offers a workable and scalable way to address FANET security issues. In order to improve intrusion detection and trust frameworks, future research will examine practical implementation, scalability for bigger UAV groups, and integrating ECC with cutting-edge technologies like blockchain and machine intelligence.

title: The Design and Research of Dynamic Calibration Device for Torque-Speed Sensor
authors: Xu Zhou, Fangjian Yuan, Qiliang Dai, Hang Xiu, Lei Wang, Yu Li, YuanPeng Lv
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Torque-speed sensors are widely used in the output torque and speed detection of rotating power equipment such as motors and generators, and their accuracy and reliability are critical to the healthy operation of power equipment. In this paper, a vertical single-axis torsional pendulum dynamic calibration device and its type selection are proposed. The device adopts servo motor to provide dynamic excitation, connects the sensor to be calibrated through flexible coupling, and eliminates friction interference with air float bearing; The rear end is connected with the self-designed replaceable weight standard inertia module, which combines the circular grating to monitor the angular displacement, angular velocity and angular acceleration in real time. A new dynamic calibration device for torque-speed sensor is provided, and the design of standard inertia module is optimized to improve the calibration accuracy.
