title: Investigation of the Effect of Different Activation Modes of KOH on the Electrochemical Properties of Water Hyacinth Biocarbon
authors: Hongli Ye, Ziyan Li, Yufei Zhou, Jianing Wang, Meiyan Li, Yan Bai
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Electrode materials with both excellent electrochemical performance and cost-effectiveness are urgently needed for the development of high-power energy storage systems. In this study, hyacinth-based biocarbon materials were prepared using a high-temperature calcination method with hyacinth as the research object. The effects of KOH activation on the electrochemical properties of hyacinth-based biochar materials prepared by immersion activation and milling activation were investigated. The results showed that the water hyacinth biochar materials prepared by milling activation assembled into electrochemical energy storage devices had the lowest equivalent series resistance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{Rs} \approx 0 \Omega)$</tex> and Rct, which ensured the minimization of energy loss and fast ion transport kinetics, exhibited excellent capacitive properties (hybrid bilayer/pseudocapacitive energy storage mechanism) corrosion resistance, and low charge-transfer resistance, which were attributed to their optimized pore structure and oxygen-functionalized surface.

title: The Impact of Smart Lighting Systems on Residential Sustainability and Evaluation through the Smart Readiness Indicator
authors: Theodora Fassa, Christina Skandali, Zoe Gareiou, Lambros T. Doulos
publish: 2025 IEEE Sustainable Smart Lighting World Conference &amp; Expo (LS25)
abstract: Smart technologies in residential environments can contribute to the optimization of both energy efficiency and quality of life. In this context, the present study investigates the role of smart lighting systems in the interaction between energy performance and user experience. The Smart Readiness Indicator (SRI) is applied to evaluate the smart lighting capabilities of a residential case study and to provide with insights into the role of lighting within the SRI framework.

title: A Design of Electric Control Brake Unit for Intelligent Delivery Vehicle on Campus
authors: Ziqi Wang, Wei Li
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: This paper begins with a brief outline of the necessity and industry prospects associated with the application of electro-mechanical brake (EMB) systems in campus delivery vehicles. A detailed description is then provided of the design methodology, braking data calculations, and control procedures developed for the EMB system of an intelligent campus transport vehicle. Although limitations are imposed on the physical implementation by the constraints of the transport vehicle itself, this work is intended to serve as a useful reference for the design and research of EMB systems.

title: Optimization and Scheduling of Integrated Energy Systems Considering Source-Load Uncertainty
authors: Chen Pang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: The integration of renewable energy and flexible loads introduces significant uncertainties to the Integrated Energy System (IES). How to overcome such uncertainties, make efficient and accurate scheduling decisions, and maintain the supplydemand balance of the entire energy system is a key challenge that the academic community is striving to address. Based on this source-load uncertainty, this paper proposes an improved Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) reinforcement learning method with dynamic noise balancing. Firstly, a mathematical model for IES scheduling is established by considering traditional power and heating supply equipment as well as flexible resources on both the source and load sides. Secondly, the IES scheduling problem is described as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), and the state space, action space, and reward function are specifically defined according to the system structure and optimization objectives. Finally, an improved TD3 algorithm based on dynamic balance of exploration noise is proposed to solve the optimized scheduling approach presented in this paper. Comparative experimental results show that the proposed algorithm reduces the economic cost by 2.36 % and 1.19 % compared with the DDPG and original TD3 algorithms, respectively. Additionally, it exhibits faster convergence speed, higher solution accuracy, and better adaptability to uncertain environments.

title: Design of Digital Control Equalization System for Ka-Band Traveling Wave Tube Amplifier
authors: YaLi Ma, YinXing Chen, Li Qiv, YiXue Wei
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: A digitally controlled equalization system solution for traveling wave tube amplifiers (TWTA) was designed, addressing the current problem of digital step attenuator (DSA) being unable to be used in TWTA. A circuit module with gain control, amplification, and shaping was designed, adapted for analog-to-digital conversion at the TWTA RF input signal. Multisim circuit simulation was used to test the circuit's performance, ensuring accurate and real-time control. A lowlevel system control program was written in C language using Keil5, performing frequency calculation, human-computer interaction, output control, and resource optimization. Finally, the system was built, and excitation signals of different frequencies and dynamic ranges were input and their data were recorded. Experimental results show that this scheme can be used to apply DSA to traveling wave tube amplifiers, with an overall delay of less than 2. 1 m s and a frequency error of less than 0. 0 2%. This scheme has strong practicality and development prospects in traveling wave tube amplifiers, and promotes the digitalization of traveling wave tube amplifiers.

title: Robustness Comparison of Scalar and Vector Control Methods in Solar-Powered Sensorless Induction Motor Applications
authors: Anand Vardhan Pandey, Anjanee Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: In this research, three various methods of control Volt/Hz control, rotor flux control, and direct torque control are evaluated for a system that delivers water which employs solar photovoltaic (SPV) arrays and induction motor drive (IMD). To ensure smooth operation, the system is designed in two stages. The first stage uses a boost converter that increases the solar photovoltaic array output voltage by dynamically modifying the duty ratio and enhance power extraction utilizing the incremental conductance method. Conversely, the second stage- DC to AC conversion for IMD-is attached to the water pumping system. Scalar control is quite easy to utilize; however, it possesses dependent torque and speed characteristics and poor dynamic flexibility. Vector control (FOC and DTC) has been utilized to enhance dynamic performance in a bid to offset scalar control's weak dynamic adaptability. Similar to a DC machine that is excited individually, the vector control provides independent torque-speed performance. The system's performance is carefully evaluated in a variety of dynamic situations, torque ripples, stator current ripples, flux response, etc. To perform the study, MATLAB/SIMULINK software models a 2.4 kW of SPV array is taken for, 2.2 kW IM fed VSI, and results shows that the corresponding usefulness of the different control strategies for solar PV water pumping systems is provided.

title: Research on Rare Image Sample Generation in Power Field Based on Flux. 1 Model
authors: Tianguang Yang, Gaoyang Zhang, Xiaona Jiang, Zixuan Wang, Hao Cui, Feng Zhao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: With the deep penetration and wide application of artificial intelligence technology in the power industry, security violation detection and equipment defect detection based on computer vision have become the key technical path to improve the intelligent level of power operation and maintenance. The performance of such detection tasks is highly dependent on highquality labeled samples. However, some scenes are sporadic, dangerous, or limited by shooting conditions, resulting in the scarcity of high-quality samples. In order to solve the problem of sample scarcity, this paper proposes a method of generating rare image samples in the power field based on flux. 1 model, which can automatically generate high-quality rare image samples when the real samples are scarce, provide a feasible scheme for the generation of rare samples in the power field, alleviate the sample dependence dilemma of artificial intelligence model, and further promote the application of intelligent detection technology in power safety production.

title: Evaluating Photovoltaic Systems in Gujarat: A Comparative Study of Annual Energy Output and LCoE Computation
authors: Dhyey Makwana, Vasu Vekariya, Siddharth Joshi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Renewable energy sources are known to have Optimistic potential in the future. These resources provide overflowing amounts of energy in the form of solar rays. In Today's life, keeping in sight the increasing load demand and also the greenhouse effect, it has the proficiency to fulfill all the required demands. This article generally studies the PV system in the commercial sector of two different locations. By various configurations of PV systems, we want to propose the Levelized Cost of Energy (LCoE), Power Distribution, and Annual Power Generation. Further to clarify the results from the PV system, we will be comparing the data obtained from two different locations (i.e., Surat and Rajkot). Thus, from the obtained data, we would be able to see the noticeable changes in the Global Irradiance and the Annual Power Generation of two different cities in the same state. The analysis from this article will help the commercial sector maximize its power output and also help reduce power inefficiencies due to the weather conditions at two different locations. This research paper demonstrates the performance model of a 2 MW Commercial PV system.

title: Application of Covariance-Optimized Multi-Sensor Fusion Technology for Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Jun Zheng, Bo Jiang, Shuangshaung Zhou
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: In terms of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) navigation systems, accurate state estimation is highly dependent on the error-state Kalman filter (ESKF) algorithm. With the increase in system state dimensionality, however, the computational complexity of covariance matrices, coupled with their storage requirements, rises sharply, thus posing challenges to real-time applications. To address this issue, an optimized covariance computation method based on sparse matrix techniques and incremental updates is proposed to significantly improve the efficiency of covariance updates. Experimental results indicate that this method is capable of maintaining the accuracy and achieving real-time performance of the ESKF algorithm on the low-cost embedded platform by greatly reducing the computational time and memory occupation. Simply put, the proposed method enhances the real-time capabilities of UAV navigation systems.

title: Research on PV-ESS Synergistic Strategy Considering Photovoltaic Uncertainty
authors: Jun Zhuang, Kuo Zhang, Jianliang Cai, Ruiyan Wang
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Addressing the coupling issues of PV -storage systems in multi-scenario scheduling within electricity markets, this paper proposes a PV-ESS synergistic strategy considering photovoltaic uncertainty. Firstly, based on the uncertainty analysis of photovoltaic output, a model for energy storage to smooth PV fluctuations is constructed. Secondly, combined with electricity price uncertainty, a synergistic strategy for energy storage systems to participate in the electricity market is proposed. Finally, through a multi-timescale synergistic framework, the charging and discharging schedule of the energy storage system is optimized by comprehensively considering the objectives of smoothing PV fluctuations, maximizing revenue in the energy market, and maximizing revenue in the frequency regulation market. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy can effectively smooth PV fluctuations and achieve maximized economic benefits in both the energy and frequency regulation markets. Specifically, while smoothing PV fluctuations, the energy storage system generates profit in the energy market through a “store low and discharge high” strategy and provides flexible ancillary services in the frequency regulation market, significantly enhancing the system's economy and stability.

title: Between Innovation and Oversight: A Cross-Regional Study of AI Risk Management Frameworks in the EU, U.S., UK, and China
authors: Amir Al-Maamari, Abdulatif Alabdultif
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Engineering (i-COSTE)
abstract: As artificial intelligence (AI) technologies increasingly enter critical sectors like healthcare, transportation, and finance, developing effective governance frameworks is crucial for managing ethical, security, and societal risks. This paper conducts a comparative analysis of AI risk management strategies across the European Union (EU), United States (U.S.), United Kingdom (UK), and China. Using a multi-method qualitative approach, we investigate how these regions classify AI risks, implement compliance, structure oversight, and respond to innovation. Findings from high-risk contexts demonstrate the advantages and limitations of different regulatory models. The EU implements a structured, risk-based framework prioritizing transparency, while the U.S. uses decentralized, sector-specific regulations that promote innovation but risk fragmented enforcement. The UK's flexible strategy facilitates agile responses but may lead to inconsistent coverage, whereas China's centralized directives allow rapid implementation while constraining public oversight. These insights highlight the need for AI regulation that is globally informed yet context-sensitive, balancing effective risk management with technological progress. We conclude with policy recommendations for enhancing effective, adaptive, and inclusive AI governance globally.

title: IoT - Based Smart Meter Attack Detection Using N - BaIoT Dataset
authors: Khushi D. Gor, Mayur H. Doshi, Yash D. Mehta, Keval N. Mehta, Saurav Verma
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Smart meters are key to smart grids and allow real-time monitoring and management of energy flow and use. They are however also subject to severe internet security threats such as Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) caused by botnets. In this paper, we propose an IoT-based attack detection framework to prevent such threats using smart meters. This study uses traffic data from the N-BaIoT dataset that consists of benign and malicious traffic produced by the Gafgyt and Mirai botnets to train our machine learning and deep learning models to identify normal and malicious smart meter activity. The outcomes indicate that our method yields a high rate of detection even with small false alarm rates, making it an efficient technique for enhancing reliability and strength in the security of smart meters in smart grid installations.

title: ML-Based Data-Driven Prediction and Sensor Placement Optimization for Thermal Safety in Electric Vehicle Batteries
authors: Aishvaria Gorityala, Sudha Radhika
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries are widely used in electric vehicles and stationary storage systems, but their safety is often compromised by localized overheating that can progress into thermal runaway. Conventional protection relies on densely placed temperature sensors, which increases wiring complexity, cost, and potential points of failure. This work proposes an integrated machine-learning approach that simultaneously identifies the most informative sensor locations and enhances fault detection accuracy using virtual temperature estimation. Various sensor-selection strategies-including mutual information analysis, permutation-based importance ranking, and active-learning-driven refinement-are examined to determine the minimum number of physical sensors required for reliable monitoring. To compensate for reduced hardware instrumentation, a hybrid convolutional and recurrent neural network is used to reconstruct temperatures at unsensed locations, enabling early detection of abnormal thermal behavior. Experiments conducted on laboratory thermalcycling data, supplemented with simulated abuse scenarios, demonstrate that the method can retain accuracy above 95% using only four to six thermistors instead of a full sensing array. This configuration lowers wiring requirements by over 40 % and identifies abnormal heating up to two minutes earlier than threshold-based methods. The approach is suitable for both newly manufactured packs and retrofit applications in EV batteries or charging-station storage units. The results confirm reliable detection of both single-cell and multi-cell thermal events, robustness under ambient variations of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 10^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, and practical suitability for retrofitted EV battery packs and charging-station storage systems.

title: Islanding Detection Using Optimized BI-LSTM Classifier with CWT for Microgrid Systems
authors: Bindu Vadlamudi, Ganta Srilakshmi, Yara Nagamalleswara Rao, Chirla satyanarayana Reddy
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Today, identifying island conditions is a significant challenge for reliable and safe operation of microgrids which are integrated with Distributed Energy Resources (DERs) such as wind and photovoltaic systems. If island conditions are not identified accurately and quickly, it can cause severe damage to equipment and to utility workers who are working on the grid. This paper presents a novel intelligent classification method for detecting islanding in microgrids using a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BI-LSTM) network. In this method, Feature extraction is conducted through the continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT), and optimal classifier weights are tuned using the whale optimization Algorithm (WOA). To identify the reliability of the proposed classifier it was evaluated under various operating scenarios which includes asymmetrical faults like line to ground (L-G), double line to ground (LL-G)and line to line (LL). In addition system voltage and current signals are tested under varying levels of noise which are measured by using signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). Although SNR is tradition- ally associated with communication systems, in this context, it serves as a metric to simulate noisy environments and assess classifier performance. These faults help the proposed model for reliable islanding detection. The proposed method was tested using the IEEE 33-bus distribution test system. It aimed to detect islanding and non-islanding events during different fault conditions. MATLAB/Simulink is implemented to confirm the proposed model performance over conventional techniques, with improved accuracy in detecting both islanding and non-islanding states.

title: Unmasking Brigading: A Network-Based Framework for Detecting Coordinated Manipulation on Reddit
authors: Aswinth Narayan A, Heman Sakkthivel M. S, Nandakumar S. Murali, Lekshmi S. Nair
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Technology, Engineering, Management for Societal impact using Marketing, Entrepreneurship and Talent (TEMSMET)
abstract: Brigading, where large groups manipulate sentiment or voting behavior across subreddits, poses a significant threat to the integrity of online platforms such as Reddit. These actions extend beyond trolling and involve complex sociotechnical interactions among communities. Grounded in the theory of symbolic boundaries, this study examines how brigading emerges structurally through clustered subreddit interactions and synchronized behavioral patterns. A computational framework is proposed using social network analysis, in which subreddit interactions are modeled as hyperlink graphs augmented with temporal and sentiment metadata. Manipulation patterns are detected and visualized in near real-time by identifying downvote bursts, sentiment alignment, and subreddit centrality. Clustering algorithms, i.e., Louvain, Girvan-Newman, and DBSCAN, are employed to uncover latent community structures often exploited for coordinated attacks. Results indicate that brigading is not a statistical anomaly but a systematic effort to breach intercommunity boundaries. To mitigate these effects, a dynamic rate-limiting mechanism is introduced, adjusting user restrictions based on the severity of the attack, the subreddit's sensitivity, and the user's prior behavior. The proposed approach contributes to maintaining platform integrity by highlighting boundary violations and enabling adaptive, real-time interventions.

title: A Fault Detection Method for Low-Voltage Transformer Areas Based on Wide-Area Response Parameters of Active Distribution Network
authors: Wenxuan Chen, Jianyue Man, Guangjun Hao, Guorong Xu, Jiarong Zhu
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: With the large-scale integration of distributed generators (DGs), fault detection in transformer areas of active distribution networks is facing new challenges. Traditional local protection exhibits insufficient sensitivity and protection blind spots due to transformer impedance isolation and the impact of high-impedance faults. To address this issue, this paper proposes a fault detection and location method for transformer areas based on wide-area response parameters in active distribution networks. First, by analyzing the synchronicity and distribution proportion of voltage sags at multiple grid connection points, the existence of a fault and its corresponding line are preliminarily determined. Subsequently, at the line level, a fault location parameter integrating the power leap rate, power fluctuation amplitude, and voltage sag rate is constructed. Based on the spatiotemporal distribution characteristics of this parameter along the line, the theoretical fault distance is determined through polynomial fitting. Finally, by combining the theoretical fault distance with the RMS current values at the feeder outlets of each transformer area, the fault determination index is calculated to achieve precise location of the faulty transformer area. Theoretical analysis and case studies demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively detect and locate transformer area faults, particularly in complex scenarios such as highimpedance faults, thereby enhancing the sensitivity and reliability of protection in active distribution networks.

title: Battery Energy Storage Supported Three-Phase Grid-Connected PV System
authors: Vineet Gautam, Rachna Garg, Kuldeep Singh
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: This paper evaluates the effectiveness of a dualstage power converter attached to a photovoltaic array with a battery energy storage system (BESS). This configuration includes the power and state controllability of the PV storage plant and increased ability to manage and strengthen the grid and the energy. The system construction consists of an inverter with 3-phases that is grid-synchronized and aC-DC Boost converter to increase the PV voltage. The battery's charging and discharging processes are handled by a bidirectional DC-DC converter (BDC), which takes into account the current generation of energy and usage. The proposed control system is designed to ensure the maximum use of the PV output with the use of the maximum power point tracking (MPPT) algorithm at the same time, while maintaining a steady DC-link voltage. The battery met the load's needs when solar generation was low and stored excess energy when production was at a peak, maintaining a stable power balance. Phase synchronization is done via a phase-locked loop (PLL). The outcome of the simulation proves the optimal functioning of the suggested system under different environmental and load dynamics. The combined operation of the solar system and the battery optimises energy usage, helps ensure the grid's stability, and extends the battery lifecycle.

title: Cloud Native Fraud Detection Frameworks Distributed AI Models for Financial and Insurance Application
authors: Narendra Narne, Aditya Koneru, Sreenivasarao Amirineni
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: The rapid development of digital financial services has not only broadened access to online transactions but also made them larger and more complex. This has made the detection of fraud in financial and insurance services a more complex task. The conventional machine learning approach is not capable of identifying complex relationships in data, which can sometimes result in incorrect predictions and a flood of false alarms. To solve this problem, a cloud-based framework for fraud detection is proposed in the study by using a combination of Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and LightGBM, which is a Light Gradient Boosting Machine. The study uses artificial data for financial transactions, which is created using a simulator known as PaySim. This simulator is used to mimic real-world transactions in mobile money services, which include transactions, payments, and withdrawals, along with fraudulent transactions. The outcomes of the experiments demonstrate that the presented framework is effective in fraud detection by outperforming conventional machine learning models such as K-Nearest Neighbors, Decision Trees, Logistic Regression, and LightGBM. The proposed framework is able to achieve 99.97% accuracy, 99.99% precision, 99.98% recall, and a 99.98% F1 score, with PR AUC and ROC AUC at 0.99 and 0.972, respectively.

title: Deep Transfer Learning Approach for Monkeypox Skin Lesion Classification via VGG16 Architecture
authors: Ramya E, Rajkumar K, Pradhiksha K, Mounesh K, Prakash P, Karthikeyan A
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Monkeypox (Mpox) is a recently identified viral infection that shares similarities with other skin ailments, complicating visual diagnosis. Rapid and precise identification of the disease is crucial to ensure prompt treatment and curb further transmission(2). This research employed a deep learning model, based on VGG16, and transfer learning to automatically categorise images of monkeypox skin lesions. A publicly available dataset consisting of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3, 1 9 2}$</tex> images, including some depicting monkeypox and others that do not, was used for both training and evaluating the model. The findings indicated that the model reached an accuracy of 87.34 %, a recall rate of 96.79 %, a precision rate of 73.02 %, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 83.56 %.The results from training and validation demonstrated reliable convergence with minimal overfitting, indicating effective performance on samples not previously encountered. While the model detected most positive cases effectively, the presence of false positives reduced precision. In future work, we will aim to improve precision by reducing false positives and comparing VGG16 with other architectures like DenseNet121 and EfficientNet to boost diagnostic performance.

title: Program Committee: LESCDT 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Logistics Engineering, Supply Chain and Digital Transformation (LESCDT)

title: Compressed Sensing for High-Speed Maneuvering Radar Targets: A Fundamental Analysis
authors: Amgad A. Salama, Syed T. Shah, Insaf Ullah, Ahmed Gamal Abdellatif, Mahmoud A. Shawky
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: This paper investigates the basic theoretical limits that are inherent in the application of compressed sensing (CS) in the detection and parameter estimation of high speed maneuvering radar targets. Through rigorous mathematical analysis, the study examines the signal properties and limitations of dictionary design and bounds in the amount of performance for reconstruction of targets with complex motion including acceleration and jerk. The analysis shows that there are serious issues to achieve the restricted isometry property (RIP) on realistic parameter spaces, because dictionary coherence exceeds practically acceptable levels. We obtain lower bounds, Cramers-Rao for parameter estimation, and show that despite theoretical computational advantages offered by the separable dictionary structures of CS, the signal features of maneuvering targets violate fundamental assumptions of CS. These results indicate that compressed sensing may not be the best framework in which to approach this class of radar problems, and hence provide a theoretical rationale for exploring other methodologies.

title: Explainable Machine Learning Models for Credit Scoring for Risk Forecasting in Big Financial Datasets
authors: Saurabh Kakkar, Bhulakshmi Makkena, Amit Chaudhary, Armaan Kakkar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Risk assessment of credit scoring should be done properly, and towards this end, sustainable lending procedures, particularly during the present digital banking age, wherein a significant portion of financial information is being calculated on a daily schedule. An explainable credit card scores machine learning model based on the XGBoost algorithm, a high-performance tool with excellent accuracy, affordability, and interpretability is presented in this study. The model uses the XGBoost algorithm, chosen for its high predictive performance and scalability. In order to facilitate fairness and interpretability and provide both global and local explanations of model behavior, explainable AI techniques such as SHAP and LIME are included. The proposed model achieves 99.71 % accuracy, 99.89 % precision, and 99.0 % F1-score, outperforming sophisticated models like CNN, AdaBoost, and Logistic Regression. The legitimacy of automated loan choices is boosted by a mix of performance and openness, and the model may be successfully applied to actual financial institutions. In general, this paper presents a strong, interpretable, and scalable credit-scoring model that meets the growing need for ethical, data-driven decision-making in the financial market.

title: Predicting Infrastructure Vulnerability to Climate Change from Street Level Imagery
authors: Grace Iordanov
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: Understanding and decreasing the vulnerability of infrastructure to climate change is paramount for climate-resilient urban planning, but descriptive assessments of vulnerability remain with limits in terms of scale and accessibility. In this paper I propose a novel-vision-based framework to predict infrastructure vulnerability from street-level images, with weakly supervised labels that are coming from datasets for geospatial climate risk. I also created a large dataset through combining Mapillary street-level images with U.S. Census tract boundaries and by overlaying other climate risk indicators such as FEMA's National Risk Index, NOAAs sea-level rise projections, and Landsat 8-derived urban heat exposure maps for making the multimodal labels for vulnerability. I will use CLIP-based visionlanguage encoders for extracting high-level semantic representations from images and training heads for lightweight regression and classification for predicting the scores for climate vulnerability. This method does not require manual annotations and allows for risk estimation at city-scale within regions that are data-sparse. The experimental results that span across five geographically diverse cities show a strong correlation amongst the scores that were predicted and ground-truth risk metrics, along with qualitative analyses indicating consistency in the alignment of important visual cues that include tree cover, building materials, and elevation-related exposure.

title: Enhancing Intrusion Detection with Transformers and Context-Aware GANs
authors: Hadil Ben Romdhane, Farah Jemili, Khaled Jouini, Ouajdi Korbaa
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: The growing sophistication of cyber threats highlights critical limitations in traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS), particularly regarding class imbalance, limited contextual understanding, and poor generalization to novel attacks. This paper introduces the RAG-GAN Transformer, a hybrid IDS architecture that integrates three key components: a conditional Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) for minorityclass data augmentation, a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) module for external context integration, and a TabTransformer encoder for structured network traffic representation. Experimental results on the LUFlow dataset demonstrate notable improvements in detection accuracy, macro F1-score, and class balance. The proposed model offers a scalable, context-aware solution for intelligent intrusion detection in diverse and imbalanced network environments.

title: Navigating Financial Instability: An Interpretable Paradigm for Multi-Label Crisis Prediction
authors: Ravjot Kaur, Gurasis Singh, Debashis Guha
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: We present a rigorous methodology for applying Elastic Weight Consolidation (EWC) to the continual prediction of banking, systemic, currency and inflation crises. Traditional models, trained on earlier regimes, are prone to catastrophic forgetting when updated on new regimes for financial crisis prediction that is a sequential, non-stationary learning problem with data arriving across time and heterogeneous jurisdictions. EWC mitigates this by constraining important parameters, quantified via a diagonal-Fisher-information approximation, to remain near previously learnt optima while permitting flexibility in less critical directions. We formalise the Bayesian derivation underpinning EWC, present the practical diagonalFisher estimator, and integrate the method into a reproducible training pipeline for multi-label crisis prediction. Our protocol uses representative task partitions, rigorous Fisher estimation, lambda scheduling, and a suite of evaluation metrics to compare performance with baseline traditional models. We provide ablation studies for Fisher estimation, penalty aggregation, and sequential walk-forward evaluation, and discuss limitations and interpretability for policy use. Our experimental model is tested for 2 years prior financial crisis prediction on Harvard Business School's global crisis database for 42 years and 70 countries, and it demonstrates ROC-AUC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 3. 1 2 \%}$</tex> in inflation crises, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 3. 5 3 \%}$</tex> in systemic crises, 77.63% in currency crises and 67.51% in banking crises predictions, outperforming the traditional models' results. We also assess the sensitivity of EWC to its core hyperparameters for real-world application. By integrating SHAP, this work aims to bridge probabilistic continual-learning theory and operational early-warning systems, making models both interpretable and robust in dynamic financial environments.

title: Forecast-Driven Portfolio Optimization: A Machine Learning GJR-GARCH-Copula Framework for High-Frequency Mean-CVaR Portfolios
authors: Zied Jelassi, Haykel Hamdi
publish: 2025 IEEE Afro-Mediterranean Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AMCAI)
abstract: This study proposes a comprehensive framework for high-frequency asset price prediction and portfolio optimization, integrating machine learning techniques with econometric models to enhance risk-adjusted returns. The study develops and compares forecasting models, including Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and Random Forests, for five assets: the S&P 500 index, Gold, Brent, the Bloomberg Commodity Index, and Bitcoin. The forecasts are coupled with volatility modeling via GJR-GARCH to account for asymmetric shocks and dependence structures estimated through Student-t copulas. These components are then integrated into a Mean-Conditional Value-at-Risk (CVaR) optimization framework, which generates portfolios tailored to varying risk appetites. The findings of the study demonstrate that forecasts enhanced by machine learning techniques significantly surpass those derived from historical data-based optimization, particularly in volatile regimes. This is evidenced by the superior risk-adjusted returns achieved by portfolios guided by LSTM and RF models. The findings of this study call into question traditional asset allocation paradigms, highlighting the critical role of dynamic dependence modeling and forward-looking volatility adjustments in modern portfolio management.

title: Tabular Data Transformation into 2D Image for CNN-Based Migraine Classification
authors: Hassiba Laifa, Assia Brek, Hakim Bendjenna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Recent Advances in Mathematics and Informatics (ICRAMI)
abstract: Migraine is a widespread neurological disorder that severely affects the quality of life for millions of people around the world. Correct migraine-type classification is an important issue for diagnosis and treatment. Therefore, this paper proposes a novel approach for migraine-type classification by transforming tabular data into a 2D image for a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model. Traditional machine learning (ML) approaches have the drawback of failing to capture the relationships between features in tabular datasets, consequently yielding a suboptimal classification performance. The proposed method tries to overcome this limitation by converting the tabular dataset into images. In that way, the CNN is able to extract the spatial patterns between features. Experimental results show that this approach significantly enhanced the migraine-type classification when compared to the classical ML model, such as XGBoost, that we have taken for benchmarking. By using the capability of CNNs for learning feature relationships, the presented approach outperformed all baseline methods.

title: Examining Big Data's Role in Shaping Monitoring Practices in the Age of Data Policing
authors: Antony Allwyn Sundarraj, Lakshya Swarup, V.P. Arul Kumar, Sanjay Bhatnagar, S. Hemelatha, Saritha Srinivasmurthy Raghotham
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Contemporary environments are characterized by the flood of surveillance infrastructures, making the exploitation of Big Data for the aggregation, scrutiny and manipulation of digitized information salient features of institutional and governmental monitoring mechanisms. This data centric paradigm generates an array of concerns including breaching of privacy, bias in algorithms and the limitations inherent on conventional forms of monitoring that are often reliant on incomplete data sets and have a mostly retrospective viewpoint. To overcome these hindrances, this study intends to present Data Policing Framework (DPF) where Big Data computational platforms such as Hadoop and Spark are focused together with probabilistic modelling specifically Bayesian inference to complement predictive monitoring and risk assessment mechanism. The framework helps in the systematized acquisition, processing, and management of large datasets that improve the acuity and timeliness of surveillance operations. Empirical evaluation of the DPF in various monitoring contexts has shown high increases in performance, which can be seen in high detection efficacies, low incidence of false positives and evidence-based decision making, which transcends the limitations of conventional methodologies. These results highlight the strong synergy that is possible in combining big-data analysis and probabilistic modelling in modern data policing activities.

title: Dynamic-Event-Based Adaptive Fuzzy Attitude Tracking Control for Six-Rotor UAV With Guaranteed Performance
authors: Xinfeng Shao, Kewen Li, Yongming Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of dynamic-event-based adaptive intelligent guaranteed performance control (GPC) for six-rotor unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) attitude systems subject to external disturbances. To enhance the generalizability of the research, the attitude system of UAVs is modeled as a multi-input multi-output (MIMO) nonlinear system with external disturbances. Leveraging fuzzy approximation technology, a fuzzy nonlinear disturbance observer (NDO) is designed to mitigate composite disturbances arising from external disturbances and fuzzy approximation errors. To ensure the transient performance of attitude tracking, a performance transformation function is introduced in conjunction with a corresponding GPC approach. A dynamic event-triggered mechanism (DETM) is developed to alleviate the communication burden, while dynamic surface control (DSC) is adopted to resolve the issue of computational complexity. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that the proposed control strategy guarantees that all signals in the closed-loop system are semi-globally uniformly ultimately bounded (SGUUB), with attitude tracking errors converging to a small residual range within the prescribed performance bounds (PPBs). Finally, applications to a six-rotor UAV attitude system validate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method.

title: EnFault: Enhanced Fault-Tolerant Physical Design of FPVA Biochips Using Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yuhan Zhu, Huayang Cai, Ren Lu, Genggeng Liu, Xing Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: With the rapid advancement of microfluidic technology towards large-scale integration, a new generation of microfluidic systems, known as Fully Programmable Valve Array (FPVA) biochips, has emerged. These biochips offer superior programmability and flexibility, making them ideal for a wide range of biochemical experiments. However, the shrinking size of chip features presents a substantial challenge in automating the physical design process. Furthermore, the occurrence of physical faults in these chips can lead to inaccurate bioassay results, further complicating the design process. The Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) algorithm has proven effective in managing the high-dimensional state space inherent in physical design tasks, enabling the identification of viable solutions even in large-scale faulty circuits. In this paper, we propose EnFault, an enhanced fault-tolerant physical design framework for FPVA biochips that addresses both placement and routing challenges in the presence of physical faults. Based on the DRL algorithm, we develop fault-tolerant placement and routing decision-making models, named DRL-FTPlacer and DRL-FTRouter, to optimize the solution quality of physical design while ensuring fault tolerance. The framework optimizes the design process by dynamically adjusting placement and routing decisions, thereby enhancing solution quality and chip reliability. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms existing algorithms in terms of completion time of bioassay and total path length of fluid transportation for both small/medium-scale and large-scale circuits. Especially in large-scale circuits, the proposed framework achieves significant improvements in solution quality, fault-tolerance performance, and execution efficiency.

title: Sensorless Control of LC-Filtered PMSM Drives Using an Easy-Tuning Full-Order Flux Observer
authors: Chao Li, Yongxiang Xu, Jibin Zou, Wentao Zhang, Shaobin Li, Shuo Chen, Zhixun Ma, Xiang Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: In LC-filtered permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drive systems, the voltage waveform at the motor terminals can be reshaped into the sinusoidal profile, thereby enhancing motor longevity. However, the introduction of the LC filter substantially increases model complexity, which necessitates an excessively high-dimensional gain matrix to guarantee observability, thereby posing considerable challenges for observer design. To address this issue, this article proposes an easy-tuning full-order flux observer (ET-FLO) for estimating the state variables and rotor position of the LC-filtered PMSM drives. By analyzing the observer error model in the frequency domain, a single-parameter tuning strategy for pole placement is derived. This strategy greatly simplifies the adjustment of the observer gain matrix, ensuring stable operation across a wide speed range. Furthermore, the adverse effect of speed estimation error on system stability has been fully investigated by constructing an error function, thereby deriving the suitable poles configuration strategy to mitigate system instability. The effectiveness of the proposed strategy has been verified through experimental results on an LC-filtered PMSM drive system.

title: The Role of Compensation and Benefits in Employee Retention: A Study in the Technology Sector
authors: Prerna, Sukhman Ghumman, Joyce R, Varsha Agarwal, Shaik Fakruddin Ali Ahmed, Manoranjan Dash
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Intelligence, Security, and Artificial Intelligence (IntelliSecAI)
abstract: Compensation and benefits are important factors that decide the fate of an employee, especially in the hotly competitive tech industry where talent mobility is high. Ensuring fairness and effectiveness of reward mechanisms with the workforce is imperative for sustainability of workforce stability and catalyzing organizational growth. Nevertheless, current approaches to assessing compensation and benefits are dominated by conventional survey or linear models, which fail to adequately address the uncertainty, subjectivity and interdependence that are inherent in the multiple factors affecting employee retention. These deficiencies reduce precision as well as strategic utility of human resource decision making. To address these gaps, this study employs Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process (F-AHP) framework that utilizes fuzzy logic and hierarchical analysis to enable a systematic evaluation of compensation and benefit components under uncertainty and sparseness of judgement. The proposed approach is applied to rank key determinants (such as salary, health benefits, stock options and flexible working policies) within a leading technology firm. This methodology will help human-resources managers determine which benefits have the greatest relative priority among employees for retaining them. Findings show that F-AHP provides better prioritization with flexible working arrangements and performance-linked incentives being key retention drivers and providing actionable insights for evidence-based HR policy. The proposed method improves the prioritization of the factors of compensation by 93 per cent, and of health benefits by by 94.5%, flexible work policies by 95%, and performance-related incentives by 95.4%.

title: Robustness of Fuzzy Logic Based DTC for DFIM in Electric Vehicle Traction under Road Slope and Wind Speed Disturbances
authors: Ahmed Chantoufi, Aziz Derouich, Najib El Ouanjli, Said Mahfoud, Abderrahman El Idrissi, Houssam Ech-Chaouy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: The transportation sector is one of the main sources of greenhouse gas emissions. This has prompted countries around the world to promote the decarbonization of this sector. This goal will be achieved by replacing internal combustion vehicles with electric vehicles (EV). To contribute to this transition, improvements to the powertrain of EV are necessary. In this context, this article presents a comparison between two control strategies used for Doubly-Fed Induction Machine (DFIM) control: conventional DTC and fuzzy logicbased DTC (FL-DTC). DFIM is used in the powertrain of EVs. This study examines DFIM operation in the face of challenges such as slope and wind variations, as well as sudden changes in EV speed (acceleration and deceleration to simulate realistic DFIM operation in EVs. The results indicate that FL-DTC offers high tracking accuracy and faster dynamic response, as well as a significant reduction in torque and power ripple compared to conventional DTC. These improvements ensure significant optimization of operational stability and efficient energy management within the traction system of EVs, confirming the relevance of FL-DTC as a robust control strategy for the propulsion of DFIM-based EVs.

title: A Context-Aware Deep Learning Framework for High-Precision Employee Turnover Forecasting
authors: G. Ramani, Lakshmi Praba V
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: Employee turnover poses significant challenges to organizational stability, performance continuity, and talent management efficiency. Traditional predictive models often fail to capture the nuanced influence of behavioral, contextual, and organizational factors underpinning attrition decisions. This paper proposes DeepAttriNet (Deep Context-Aware Attrition Prediction Network), a context-aware deep learning framework designed to enhance employee turnover forecasting accuracy. The framework processes employee attributes, engagement indicators, performance records, and organizational structure variables through parallel encoding networks, allowing it to learn latent relationships and temporal behavior patterns. An attention-based fusion layer then prioritizes influential features, improving interpretability and prediction precision. Experiments conducted on real-world HR datasets demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms conventional machine learning and standard deep neural network classifiers in terms of F1-score, precision, and recall. The model also provides interpretable insights into contextual drivers behind employee turnover, supporting data-informed workforce retention strategies.

title: A Comprehensive Regression Framework for Photovoltaic Systems: From Data Analysis to Real-Time Notifications
authors: Mohamed Karom, Zakia Hasnaoui, Meryem Yahyaoui, Abdellah Kadaoui, Chaieb El Bekkaye, Abdelouahad Tahani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Technologies and Interdisciplinary Innovation (ICAT2I)
abstract: This paper suggests a comprehensive and costeffective photovoltaic maintenance system intended to meet the growing demand for smart monitoring solutions in the market. The approach integrates various processes for data collection and analysis, regression modeling, real-time anomaly detection, and automated notification capabilities. The key objective is to enhance remote monitoring, data management, and fault detection by leveraging both historical meteorological and power databases. The workflow begins with the use of two wellestablished representatives of Machine Learning paradigms to produce regression models based on eight variables. Correlation analysis was then applied for feature selection to reduce computational load without compromising overall quality. This action leads to a refined and meaningful subset that was assessed against the first full model using well-known metrics such as the root mean squared error and the coefficient of determination. The results show that the optimized model maintains high accuracy while streamlining the protocol for rapid execution. Finally, to operationalize the method, a unified architecture for frequent data collection was implemented. The pipeline feeds a fault detection mechanism that tracks disparities between projected and real power and flags potential under performance in realtime, prompting appropriate physical inspections. This end-toend setup bridges renewable energy practical applications with the field of Machine Learning. It was specifically built for operators with limited technical expertise, introducing a versatile and affordable strategy to improve the reliability of PV systems.

title: VisuaQuery: An AI-Powered Natural Language to SQL and Visualization System
authors: Babul Mandal, Aditya Raut, Pranav Hopal, Pushkar Raut, Raunak Kalamkhede, Sanchit Yelne, Sandesh Shinde
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Engineering and Medical Sciences (ICETEMS)
abstract: This paper introduces a Multimodal NLP-to-SQL and Visualization system. It is a Database Interaction System that facilitates database communication through text, image, and voice inputs without SQL knowledge. The system integrates OCR, speech-to-text, and a transformer-based Large Language Model (LLM) to transform user queries to accurate schemaaware SQL statement. A validation layer ensures query safety and correctness before execution. Experimental evaluation demonstrates high query accuracy, low response latency, and robust performance across modalities. The proposed approach improves accessibility, efficiency, and usability of database systems, making data retrieval more inclusive for non-technical users.

title: Predicting Live Commerce Conversion in Indonesia with Machine Learning
authors: Sabar Aritonang Rajagukguk, Timothy Dillan, Ady Ekayana Putra
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Live commerce, or shoppertainment, is rapidly transforming digital retail in Indonesia, yet conversion outcomes remain difficult to predict. This study develops a machine learning framework that integrates behavioral analytics to model conversion drivers in Indonesian live commerce sessions. Using a large-scale dataset of 1.2 million interactions, we extract behavioral features such as comment velocity, flash sale frequency, and host responsiveness. Among tested models, XGBoost achieved the strongest performance (AUC = 0.924, accuracy = 89.3%), significantly outperforming baselines. Feature interpretability analysis with SHAP values highlights the central role of interaction intensity and promotional strategies. Real-world validation with 23 SME partners confirmed the model’s practical utility, yielding an average 34.7% increase in conversion rates and a 1,254% ROI. The findings demonstrate the value of behavioral-level prediction for optimizing live commerce strategies and provide a foundation for future research integrating multimodal and cross-cultural perspectives.

title: Lagrange–Chebyshev Interpolated CNN–LSTM Framework for ECG Arrhythmia Classification
authors: Wandi Yusuf Kurniawan, Putu Harry Gunawan, Norma Binti Alias, Narita Aquarini, Muhammad Ilyas
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Accurate electrocardiogram (ECG) interpretation is crucial for early arrhythmia detection, but it remains hindered by noise and morphological distortion. This study proposes a Lagrange–Chebyshev–based Convolutional Long Short-Term Memory (CNN–LSTM) framework that unifies numerical interpolation with deep temporal learning for robust ECG reconstruction and classification. Signals from the MIT–BIH Arrhythmia Database were denoised, segmented, and reconstructed using Lagrange–Chebyshev interpolation to suppress endpoint oscillations and preserve diagnostic morphology before classification into the AAMI five-class taxonomy. The integrated CNN–LSTM model achieved strong overall performance (accuracy 0.9691, macro F1 0.9315, macro AUC 0.9929), demonstrating reliable discrimination across arrhythmia types with low computational cost. Unlike prior deep or attention-based methods, this framework enhances ECG interpretability by coupling mathematically grounded waveform reconstruction with data-driven temporal modeling, improving both morphological fidelity and model generalization. The approach is compact enough for near-real-time inference, highlighting its potential for embedded or wearable diagnostic applications. This framework thus establishes a mathematically interpretable and scalable foundation for future real-time arrhythmia detection systems.

title: A Systematic Literature Review of Semi-Supervised Learning on Time-Series Data
authors: Fahris Mumtaza Ahsan, Igi Ardiyanto, Sunu Wibirama
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Time-series data are widely utilized in fields such as healthcare, industry, finance, and energy, yet their effective analysis remains constrained by the scarcity of labeled data. Semi-supervised learning (SSL) has emerged as a promising paradigm that integrates limited labeled data with abundant un-labeled samples to enhance model generalization and robustness. This study presents a comprehensive systematic literature review (SLR) of 93 peer-reviewed studies published between 2021 and 2024, focusing on SSL methodologies, application domains, and evaluation strategies in time-series analysis. The review identifies pseudo-labeling as the most prevalent approach due to its simplicity and flexibility, while recent research trends highlight the growing adoption of advanced methods such as contrastive learning, graph-based models, generative adversarial networks (GANs), and ensemble frameworks including MixMatch, Fix-Match, and SelfMatch. These approaches demonstrate improved capability in handling temporal dependencies, missing data, and noisy observations. SSL has been successfully applied across diverse domains, including medical diagnosis, industrial fault detection, energy forecasting, and remote sensing. Accuracy remains the dominant evaluation metric, but complementary measures such as F1-score, recall, AUC, and regression-based errors are increasingly employed for fairer assessments. Overall, SSL exhibits strong potential to enhance time-series modeling under limited supervision, with current trends moving toward hybrid, domain-adapted, and interpretable frameworks that bridge methodological rigor and real-world applicability.

title: Prediction Augmented Deep Reinforcement Learning Framework for Autonomous Vehicle Lane-Change Decision Making
authors: Jianquan Chen, Cheng Peng, Zhen Wang, Tao Gao
publish: 2025 9th International Conference on Electrical, Mechanical and Computer Engineering (ICEMCE)
abstract: Reliable lane-change decision making remains a key challenge for autonomous driving, particularly under partial observability where the intentions of surrounding vehicles are uncertain. Most deep reinforcement learning (DRL) approaches rely solely on instantaneous observations, which limits foresight and may lead to unsafe or inefficient maneuvers. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a Perception-Prediction-Decision DRL framework that integrates perception, prediction, and decision making into a unified architecture. A lightweight prediction module is employed to provide short-term trajectory prediction of surrounding vehicles, thereby enriching the agent's observation space with future context. The framework is implemented with PPO and SAC algorithms, and the performance is evaluated through extensive simulations in CARLA. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed framework markedly improves lane-change performance, with PPO achieving higher lane-change success rates and significantly lower collision rates compared to baselines, thereby suggesting the benefit of embedding prediction into the DRL decision-making.

title: Gigarad Ionizing Radiation-Induced Carrier Mobility Degradation in 28 Nm Commercial Cmos
authors: Farzan Jazaeri, Ashkhen Yesayan, Amin Rassekh, Jean-Michel Sallese
publish: 2025 IEEE Latin American Electron Devices Conference (LAEDC)
abstract: This paper presents an experimental study on the impact of total ionizing dose (TID) damage on free carrier mobility in a commercial 28 nm bulk CMOS technology, with exposure levels reaching up to 1 Grad. The analysis is based on a proposed, accurate methodology for extracting free carrier mobility, which assumes drift-diffusion transport in advanced field-effect transistors [1], [2]. Using this approach, we investigate the degradation of carrier mobility caused by Coulomb scattering from radiation-induced interface trapped charges. The study provides new insights into the physical mechanisms behind mobility reduction in deeply scaled nodes under high-dose radiation. The extracted mobility trends offer valuable benchmarks for reliability assessment and radiation-hardened design in nanoscale CMOS technologies.

title: Dynamic LSTM-Based Energy Consumption Prediction for Smart Home Applications
authors: Kiran Saripudi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: The increasing integration of Internet of Things (IoT) devices and advanced measurement systems in smart homes has amplified the need for accurate short-term energy consumption forecasting. Reliable load prediction enables intelligent energy management, cost optimization, and improved demand-response strategies for residential environments. This study investigates the performance of three deep learning architectures—Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and Dynamic LSTM (D-LSTM)—for predicting household energy demand using historical consumption data. The proposed D-LSTM model is designed to adapt to fluctuating consumption patterns in smart homes, capturing both short- and long-term temporal dependencies with high precision. Experimental results, derived from real-world residential power consumption datasets, demonstrate that D-LSTM outperforms traditional LSTM and GRU models in forecasting accuracy and peak demand detection. The findings highlight the potential of D-LSTM-based models to enable proactive energy control, support renewable integration, and enhance the operational efficiency of smart home energy management systems.

title: Monitoring Olive Tree Water Status in Semi-Arid Regions Using Dendrometry and Sap Flow Measurements
authors: Abdelhafid Elallaoui, Saïd Khabba, Salah Er-Raki, Adnane Chakir, Valérie Le Dantec, Pierre-Louis Frison, Lionel Jarlan
publish: 2025 International Congress on Smart Agriculture and Sustainable Systems (SmartAgri&amp;SuSY)
abstract: In semi-arid regions, irrigation accounts for more than 85% of total water use, often resulting in inefficient water management and overexploitation of groundwater resources. Therefore, the rational use of irrigation water requires accurate monitoring of plant water status to optimize water application and maintain crop productivity. Among the available approaches, dendrometric and sap flow measurements provide powerful, complementary tools for assessing the dynamic water balance of trees. Dendrometric measurements, based on high-resolution monitoring of trunk radius variations, offer valuable insights into short-term fluctuations in plant water status and long-term growth dynamics. These variations reflect reversible daily cycles of trunk shrinkage and swelling associated with water storage and depletion in living tissues. In this study, we investigated the potential of the nighttime trunk radius increment slope derived from dendrometric data as an indicator of olive tree water status under semi-arid conditions. To this end, dendrometers recording trunk radius variations every 5 minutes and seven heat dissipation probes (TDP) measuring sap flow every 30 minutes were installed on olive trees located in the Chichaoua region of the Haouz plain, Morocco. In parallel, meteorological parameters and soil moisture were continuously monitored to provide complementary environmental data. The results showed that the nighttime stem radius increment slope was significantly correlated with both vapor pressure deficit (VPD) and sap flow, demonstrating its sensitivity to variations in plant water status. The results suggest that the nighttime trunk radius increment slope is a promising physiological indicator of tree water status under semi-arid conditions.

title: A Hybrid Transformer-Based Approach for Optimal Multimodal Phishing Detection
authors: Vidhula Thomas, Anubhav Sharma, Manmohan Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Phishing websites continue to be a threat because of the misleading impression they give and increasing sophistication. A phishing website is a site that imitates a legitimate website, convincing users to provide their sensitive information. Traditional approaches to detect phishing websites based on URLs and/or HTML no longer work because phishing approaches continue to evolve. This evolution led to the emergence of visual similarity detection approaches that attempt to detect phishing websites based on visual similarities to legitimate websites. These visual similarity-based methods can often result in high false positive detection rates, but are also still impacted by everchanging URLs and impersonated entities. Consequently, this publication leverages a state-of-the-art multimodal method using a hybrid transformer and deep learning techniques for detecting phishing websites. The model is further developed using pre-trained architectures, where the model provides a local and semantic representation of the URL and HTML DOM graphs. Features are selected using a modified horned lizard optimization (MHLO) method to deal with the high-dimensional data and increase model efficiency. The classification model is a wavelet deep belief neural network (W-DBNN) to reliably separate phishing websites from legitimate websites. The simulation results on the Common Crawl, Phishtank, and Mendeley datasets illustrate how the proposed MHLO+W-DBNN model is effective. It performs well across several public datasets. The proposed MHLO + W-DBNN multimodal phishing detection model integrates transformer-based feature extraction with enhanced deep learning classification. It attained an accuracy of 98.856 %, precision of 97.105 %, recall of 98.328 %, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 97.713 %, surpassing current CNN, Transformer, and GCN models by as much as 11.63 %. The Modified Horned Lizard Optimization (MHLO) effectively identified high-impact features, enhancing performance and minimizing computational burden. By amalgamating URL and HTML modalities, the model apprehends both semantic and structural phishing indicators, thereby augmenting its resilience against advancing attack patterns. This research suggests a highly accurate, adaptable, and generalizable detection approach, providing a robust basis for AI-based phishing defense systems in real-world cybersecurity applications.

title: IndoorNavX: ARCore-Powered Markerless Indoor Navigation
authors: Meer Mehta, Kushal Mehta, Kushagra Chauhan, Monika Mangla, Monali Sankhe, Pravin Hole
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Smart Power, Energy, Renewables, and Transportation (SPERT)
abstract: Route planning between spatial locations constitutes a fundamental requirement for autonomous systems and robotic platforms. Satellite-based positioning systems effectively serve outdoor environments; however, enclosed architectural spaces including commercial complexes, medical facilities, and transportation hubs present significant localization challenges due to satellite signal attenuation within building structures. Consequently, conventional interior positioning approaches depend on infrastructure-based technologies such as Quick Response markers, Bluetooth Low Energy proximity sensors, and wireless signal mapping. These methods demonstrate scalability constraints stemming from extensive hardware deployment requirements and maintenance overhead. Addressing these constraints, this research presents a feature-based Augmented Reality framework for interior wayfinding, implemented within the Android development environment utilizing Google's ARCore spatial computing library and Sceneform rendering toolkit. The developed solution integrates surface recognition algorithms, continuous motion estimation, and Visual-Inertial Odometry for environmental spatial interpretation, delivering instantaneous three-dimensional directional guidance to users. Environmental understanding capabilities enable predefined route storage through virtual anchor positioning combined with multi-sensor data fusion, facilitating offline operational capacity. A streamlined interaction interface presents vectorized directional indicators, waypoint markers, and terminal location arrows, enhancing overall user engagement quality. The suggested system has the capability to use its numerous applications from smart campuses to AR-based building management.

title: Dynamic Bus Scheduling Using Transformer-Based Demand Forecasting
authors: Shreesh Gawande, Rishi Mishra, Jay Kumar Vagairya, Rahul S, Prasoon Kumar Bharati, Nidhi Meshram, Veena Anand
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Inefficiencies in urban bus scheduling, mainly driven by changing passenger demand, cause a high level of waste in operations and a poor perceived customer experience. Static schedules often lead to a mismatch between the passenger demand and scheduled supply. This looks like either full buses during peak periods or under-utilized “ghost buses” during off-peak periods. This paper describes the design and implementation of an intelligent forecasting system for the adaptive transit landscape of New York City. We introduce a new multivariate dataset built from four years of historical MTA bus ridership data combined with GTFS route specifications, citywide traffic behavior, and weather data. At the heart of the proposed system is a Transformer-based neural network used to model long-range dependencies in time-series data, which will forecast hourly passenger volume. Additionally, the system has a robust backend API and a dynamic dispatch algorithm that creates a methodology for converting raw predictions into scheduling recommendations. The model shows performance on the test set with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.91 and a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 23.05. This work serves as a proof-of-concept for a from reactive to a predictive, data-driven public transit operations.

title: Integrative Network Pharmacology and Molecular Dynamics Uncover Monoterpenoid-Based Inhibitors Targeting Aurora Kinase B in Prostate, Testicular, and Bladder Cancers
authors: Satya Vivek Jaiswal, Nidhi Mishra
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)
abstract: Aurora kinase B (AURKB) plays a critical role in maintaining chromosomal integrity and regulating mitosis, which has emerged as a potential target in prostate, testicular, and bladder cancers. In this study, the transcriptomics dataset from these malignancies was analyzed to identify the commonly upregulated genes, followed by protein-protein interaction (PPI) network analysis, which revealed that AURKB was the top-ranked hub gene based on betweenness centrality. Monoterpenoid compounds from PubChem were subjected to structure-based virtual screening against AURKB using docking. The lead compound (PubChem ID 16732680) exhibits a higher docking score (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9.178 \text{kcal} / \text{mol}$</tex>) than the reference compound, enzalutamide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.495 \text{kcal} / \text{mol}$</tex>), and demonstrates favourable drug-likeness properties. The stability of the AURKB-ligand complex was further evaluated through a 100-nanosecond molecular dynamics simulation. These findings suggest that monoterpenoids may serve as a potential candidate for targeting AURKB, highlighting their therapeutic relevance for male-specific cancers.

title: Design and Implementation of a Low-Cost Automatic Weather Station Based on the Internet of Things
authors: Sefia Attia, Abdelkader Mechernene, Mourad Loucif
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: This project involves creating a system for collecting and displaying measurements from an automated and cost-effective weather station designed for renewable energy installations. It is part of a monitoring strategy based on the Internet of Things (IoT). The prototype is energyautonomous and powered by small solar panels and lithiumpolymer batteries, via an MCP1700-LDO (low-dropout voltage) regulator. Environmental data, such as ambient temperature, solar radiation intensity, wind speed and direction, UV index, etc., are collected without human intervention using various sensors. The station is located on the roof of the research laboratory building of the Faculty of Technology at the University of Tlemcen. The Stevenson shield was entirely designed and manufactured using 3D printing to protect the station. The data is collected and transmitted by an ESP32 board equipped with a Tensilica Xtensa LX6 processor and a Wi-Fi module. This board offers excellent value for money for IoT projects. It is programmable with several programming languages, such as C/C++ or Micro-Python, thanks to its various libraries. The ThingSpeak platform was chosen to store the collected information online (cloud), display it on smartphones, tablets and computers, and predict the temperature using the LSTM deep learning model to evaluate the station's performance and reliability.

title: Comparative Study on Optimal Directional Overcurrent Relay Coordination Using Linear and Nonlinear Approaches
authors: Raghvendra Tiwari, Devesh Jaiswal, Raj Kapur Kumar, Biranchi Narayan Kar, Roshan Chitranshi, Amiya Kumar Naik
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The primary goal of relay coordination is to determine the optimal relay configurations that minimize total operating time while ensuring proper coordination between each relay pair. This paper presents a comparative analysis of linear and nonlinear programming techniques for achieving optimal directional overcurrent relay (DOCR) coordination. The coordination problem is formulated as a constrained optimization task, incorporating both linear and nonlinear models. The effectiveness of the proposed coordination schemes is evaluated using different relay characteristics, including normal inverse (NI), very inverse (VI), extremely inverse (EI), and custom-defined curves. The Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO) is applied to obtain the optimal settings, and its performance is compared against that of the Genetic Algorithm (GA). To demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed approach, the distribution part of the IEEE-14 bus test system (7-bus microgrid test system) is considered.

title: Sustainable Power Generation via Olive Solid Waste Gasification in the Tizi Ouzou Region
authors: Abdenour Elias, Abderrezak Kennas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems, and Renewable Energy (AIESRE)
abstract: Each year, mills in the Kabylia region generate thousands of tons of olive-wastes, which is typically discarded or burnt in fields. This study addresses the valorisation of olive solid waste through gasification. A theoretical study and simulation was conducted on a small-scale plant designed for heat and power generation. The plant consists of a downdraft gasifier, an externally-fired gas turbine, and an organic Rankine cycle. The syngas produced in the gasification process showed the following molar composition: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H}_{2} 16.86 \%$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO} 20.66 \%$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CH}_{4} 1.62 \%, \text{CO}_{2} 9.4 \%$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}_{2} 50.7 \%$</tex>. The syngas had an LHV of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.71 \text{MJ} / \text{kg}$</tex> and a gasification efficiency of 72.15%. When supplied with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$112 ~\text{kg} / \mathrm{h}$</tex> of olive pomace, the EFGT generated an electrical output of 75.8 kWe. The flue gases exiting the turbine powered the organic Rankine cycle, facilitating the generation of both electrical and thermal energy. This study evaluates the performance of six working fluids: cyclohexane, isopentane, R113, benzene, R245fa, and methanol. Among these fluids, Isopentane demonstrated the highest electrical power output, producing 35.4 kWe and achieving a combined total output of 111.2 kWe. This resulted in an improvement in electrical efficiency from 18.3% to 24.07%. Based on the thermodynamic performances, a thermo-economic evaluation was conducted, incorporating the sale of both electricity and thermal energy in the form of hot water.
